jobs.title: Deputy Director
jobs.description:
**Overview**

For 50 years, Commonweal has worked at the intersection of health, healing, education, environmental stewardship, and social change. Through a diverse portfolio of programs, we bring together researchers, practitioners, artists, educators, healthcare professionals, environmental leaders, and community partners to address some of today's most complex challenges.

Just as important as what we do is how we work.

At Commonweal, we value curiosity, thoughtful leadership, and genuine collaboration. We believe the strongest organizations are built on trust, respect, open communication, and a willingness to learn from one another. Our staff work across disciplines and programs, supporting each other's success while remaining deeply committed to our individual missions. We strive to create an environment where people are encouraged to contribute ideas, take initiative, build strong relationships, and do meaningful work alongside colleagues who care deeply about making a difference.

As we continue to grow and evolve, we're seeking an experienced **Deputy Director** to help lead Commonweal's next chapter.

Reporting to the Executive Director, the Deputy Director serves as Commonweal's senior internal leader and strategic partner, providing leadership across finance, human resources, operations, technology, administration, organizational development, and program support. This role helps strengthen organizational systems, develop leaders, improve operational effectiveness, and ensure that Commonweal's infrastructure supports the important work happening across our programs.

We're looking for someone who:

* Has significant senior leadership experience managing complex organizations or departments.
* Brings strong operational, financial, and organizational management skills.
* Is an experienced people leader who enjoys coaching others and building high\-performing teams.
* Thinks strategically while remaining practical and solution\-oriented.
* Is comfortable leading organizational change while preserving a collaborative and respectful culture.
* Values relationships, clear communication, and thoughtful decision\-making.
* Is excited by the opportunity to help a mission\-driven nonprofit strengthen its organizational capacity for the future.

Why Commonweal?

This is an opportunity to help lead an organization with an extraordinary history and an ambitious future. You'll partner closely with the Executive Director and work alongside dedicated colleagues who are committed to improving health, protecting the environment, and advancing social change. You'll have the opportunity to shape systems, strengthen leadership, and help ensure that Commonweal continues to thrive for decades to come.

Position Details

* **Location:** Hybrid. Regular on\-site work at Commonweal's headquarters in Bolinas, California, with flexibility for remote work when appropriate.
* **Salary:** $175,000–$205,000 annually, depending on experience.
* **Status:** Full\-time, Regular, Exempt.
* **Benefits:** Comprehensive benefits package including employer\-sponsored health, dental, vision, life and long\-term disability insurance; generous paid time off; retirement benefits; and additional benefits based on eligibility.

**If you're inspired by Commonweal's mission and excited to help strengthen an organization where thoughtful leadership, collaboration, and service are central to our culture, we'd love to hear from you.** 

View the complete Job Description: Deputy Director, Commonweal

https://drive.google.com/file/d/15f9BQGvo0kLteTPxQTyqoVS7IVS5kvE\_/view

To apply, please send your resume and a cover letter to jobs@commonweal.org.

Pay: $175,000\.00 \- $205,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 403(b)
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible schedule
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Bolinas, CA 94924

jobs.title: Chief Program Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Program Officer****ABOUT US**

CollegeSpring is the only national nonprofit providing teacher\-led test prep during the school day, helping students impacted by poverty unlock greater options after high school. For 16 years, we’ve partnered with school districts and organizations across the country to help more than 100,000 primarily first generation, economically disadvantaged, BIPOC students unlock opportunity through increased scores on gateway tests like the SAT, ACT, and TSIA2 (Texas Success Initiative Assessment). Today, we continue to expand by maximizing impact through larger district and nonprofit partnerships and harnessing the power of technology to better serve teachers and students.

**ABOUT THE ROLE**

At CollegeSpring, everything we do ultimately serves one purpose: improving student outcomes.


As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, the Chief Program Officer (CPO) shapes the long\-term vision and strategy for CollegeSpring's programs, connecting instructional excellence, evidence, innovation, organizational capacity, partnerships, and technology to maximize student impact.


This is a transformational leadership opportunity to shape the next chapter of CollegeSpring's growth and impact. The CPO will lead a national program organization of 12 team members, including three Directors, responsible for delivering high\-quality programs today while building the next generation of CollegeSpring's offerings. In FY27, CollegeSpring will serve approximately 55,000 students across more than 100 schools in 15 states, with an ambitious goal of reaching 250,000 students annually by 2030\. The CPO will play a central role in achieving that vision by strengthening existing programs, developing new products and services, and expanding CollegeSpring's impact.


Success in this role will be measured by the Chief Program Officer's ability to improve student outcomes while strengthening CollegeSpring's long\-term organizational growth and impact. Key results include:


* 100% of participating students increase their assessment scores.
* 90% of participating students meet college readiness benchmarks.
* 90% of participating students increase test confidence.
* 85% partner retention through exceptional program quality and partner experience.

**WHAT YOU’LL DO**

**Program Vision \& Innovation:** Steward the long\-term excellence and evolution of CollegeSpring's program, ensuring it continues to deepen student impact while responding to the evolving needs of educators, partners, and the education landscape.

* Program Vision: Define the long\-term vision and strategic direction for CollegeSpring's instructional model, program portfolio, curriculum, and technology ecosystem.
* Program Excellence: Continuously strengthen the quality, innovation, effectiveness, and scalability of CollegeSpring's instructional model and program portfolio to improve student outcomes.
* Program Innovation: Identify and lead strategic innovations, including new curriculum, services, instructional models, and the thoughtful application of AI and emerging technologies that expand CollegeSpring's impact and position the organization to serve significantly more students over time.

**Executive Leadership:** Provide executive leadership that aligns strategy, people, investments, and organizational priorities to maximize student impact.

* Strategic Leadership: Serve as a member of the Executive Leadership Team, shaping organizational priorities, investment decisions, resource allocation, and long\-term planning. Integrate data, program outcomes, educator and partner feedback, operational realities, financial considerations, and emerging trends to evaluate opportunities, navigate competing priorities, and make strategic decisions that maximize long\-term student impact.
* Leadership Development: Lead and develop a national program organization of Directors and program leaders, building the organizational capacity required to scale CollegeSpring's impact while maintaining program quality and student outcomes.
* Mission Stewardship: Champion CollegeSpring's mission, values, and commitment to educational equity while ensuring every strategic decision advances measurable student outcomes.

**Strategic Partnerships \& Organizational Growth:** Cultivate the relationships, influence, and opportunities that expand CollegeSpring's reach, strengthen its reputation, and advance its mission.


* Strategic Partnerships: Cultivate relationships with school systems, nonprofit organizations, funders, higher education institutions, state agencies, and strategic partners that advance the mission.
* Growth Strategy: Identify and pursue opportunities to expand CollegeSpring's reach through new markets, program offerings, funding opportunities, and strategic collaborations.
* External Leadership: Represent CollegeSpring nationally through thought leadership, speaking engagements, publications, and participation in education networks.




**HOW YOU’LL DO IT**

* **Strategic Thinking and Decision Making:** You think at the systems level, connecting instructional excellence, evidence, stakeholder needs, organizational capacity, and emerging opportunities into a cohesive long\-term strategy. You make thoughtful strategic tradeoffs, determine where the organization should invest, innovate, scale, refine, or discontinue efforts, and translate vision into actionable plans that maximize student outcomes.
* **People Leadership:** You build high\-performing organizations by developing senior leaders, aligning talent and resources around strategic priorities, and fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. You empower teams to execute strategy while creating the organizational capacity needed to sustain excellence and growth.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
**Department:** Executive Leadership  
**Employment Type:** Full\-Time, Salaried  
**Salary Range:** **$124,000 – $149,000 annually** (Based on qualifications, experience, and performance)

Position Summary

LaunchPad Solutions is seeking an accomplished, visionary, and results\-driven **Chief Executive Officer (CEO)** to lead our company into its next phase of growth. This executive will be responsible for establishing strategic direction, driving revenue growth, developing organizational leadership, and ensuring operational excellence across all divisions of the business.

We are looking for a leader with a proven history of building businesses, scaling operations, increasing profitability, and developing high\-performing teams. The ideal candidate will possess executive leadership experience, exceptional business acumen, and the confidence to make sound decisions that position LaunchPad Solutions as an industry leader.

Whether your experience comes from serving as a CEO, President, Chief Operating Officer, Managing Director, Founder, or another executive leadership role, we are looking for someone with the knowledge, vision, and execution skills to help accelerate our company's growth.

Key Responsibilities

* Develop and execute the company's long\-term strategic vision.
* Lead organizational growth, expansion, and market development initiatives.
* Oversee all company operations, departments, and executive leadership.
* Establish annual business goals, budgets, and key performance indicators (KPIs).
* Drive revenue growth while maintaining profitability and operational efficiency.
* Build, mentor, and lead a high\-performing executive and management team.
* Foster a culture of accountability, innovation, collaboration, and excellence.
* Evaluate new business opportunities, strategic partnerships, and acquisitions.
* Monitor financial performance and implement strategies to maximize profitability.
* Present company performance updates to ownership and stakeholders.
* Ensure compliance with all federal, state, and local regulations.
* Develop organizational policies and procedures that support sustainable growth.
* Represent LaunchPad Solutions with clients, investors, partners, and the public.
* Make executive decisions regarding organizational priorities, investments, staffing, and expansion.
* Identify emerging market trends and position the company for long\-term success.

Required Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Finance, Management, Entrepreneurship, or a related field (Master's degree or MBA preferred).
* Five or more years of executive leadership experience in a CEO, President, COO, Executive Director, or similar senior leadership role.
* Demonstrated success leading business growth, improving operations, or expanding market presence.
* Strong understanding of finance, budgeting, strategic planning, and business operations.
* Experience leading and developing executive leadership teams.
* Outstanding communication, negotiation, and decision\-making skills.
* Proven ability to manage multiple departments while maintaining organizational focus.
* High ethical standards, professionalism, and integrity.

Preferred Qualifications

* Experience scaling startup or growth\-stage companies.
* Experience in business development, sales leadership, or operations management.
* Knowledge of corporate governance and organizational development.
* Background in technology, business services, consulting, or business process outsourcing is a plus.
* Experience implementing systems, processes, and performance management frameworks.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities.

Leadership Competencies

The successful CEO will demonstrate:

* Visionary leadership
* Strategic thinking
* Financial acumen
* Organizational development
* Change management
* Executive decision\-making
* Team building and mentorship
* Operational excellence
* Business development expertise
* Relationship management
* Integrity and accountability
* Innovation and adaptability

Performance Expectations

The Chief Executive Officer will be expected to:

* Develop and execute measurable growth strategies.
* Increase company revenue while maintaining profitability.
* Build a high\-performing leadership team.
* Improve operational efficiency across all departments.
* Strengthen company culture and employee engagement.
* Expand market presence and strategic partnerships.
* Maintain financial discipline and responsible corporate governance.
* Position LaunchPad Solutions for sustainable, long\-term success.

Compensation \& Benefits

* Annual Salary: **$124,000 – $149,000**
* Executive performance bonus opportunities
* Paid vacation and company holidays
* Professional development and executive education opportunities
* Career advancement and long\-term leadership opportunities
* Opportunity to shape the future of a rapidly growing organization

Why Join LaunchPad Solutions?

LaunchPad Solutions is committed to helping businesses grow through innovative solutions, operational excellence, and outstanding client service. As Chief Executive Officer, you will have the opportunity to shape the company's future, build a lasting legacy, and lead an organization with ambitious goals and unlimited growth potential. We are seeking a leader who thrives on innovation, embraces challenges, and is passionate about building exceptional companies.

**LaunchPad Solutions is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applications from qualified candidates of all backgrounds.**

Pay: $124,427\.63 \- $149,848\.31 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Los Angeles, CA 90064

jobs.title: Executive Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**About Destination Tomorrow** 

Destination Tomorrow (DT) is a national grassroots LGBTQ\+ organization founded in the Bronx, built on the principle that no individual should have to leave their neighborhood for necessary services. DT's mission is to move clients off the path of emergency care to one where they can grow, learn, and flourish by emphasizing economic, social, and mental empowerment through comprehensive educational, financial, and health programs, as well as housing solutions. As an expanding multi\-site organization, Destination Tomorrow proudly serves the community across The Bronx, New York City; Atlanta, GA; and Washington, D.C.

**Position Summary**

**The Executive Director of Operations (ED Ops)** is Destination Tomorrow's enterprise infrastructure leader, translating strategic vision into operational architecture—aligning the executive team and building the internal capacity, accountability systems, analytics capabilities, compliance and risk governance frameworks necessary to support enterprise\-wide excellence and sustainable expansion. As the agency grows, the ED Ops ensures that workforce systems and process discipline shift from ad hoc execution to structured, repeatable, and enforceable.

Building on the agency's existing design, under the direction of the CEO and CSO, the ED Ops develops a cross\-agency operational framework that replicates the DT Community Model across office locations and client sites. Specific requirements for housing plug into this framework. Day\-to\-day housing operations remain with Social Services leadership.

**Core Responsibilities**

**Executive Operating Model \& Meeting Cadence** 

* Establish and facilitate the enterprise executive operating rhythm, eliminating fragmentation across verticals and ensuring infrastructure planning aligns with strategic initiatives, revenue campaigns, and major organizational milestones.
* Introduce standardized governance, reporting, and accountability processes across operational and programmatic leadership.
* Ensure executive dashboards include performance metrics, risk summaries, workforce indicators, and operational health signals, and are integrated into QBRs, Board reporting cycles, and enterprise risk reviews.
* Partner with the CEO, CSO, and Executive Director of Social Services (EDSS) to translate 3\- and 5\-year strategic plans into operational roadmaps covering staffing, facilities, technology, systems, and sustained budgeting.
* Partner with the EDSS to ensure enterprise systems, reporting structures, workforce infrastructure, and operational governance effectively support frontline program delivery and client services.

**Enterprise Operating Framework \& Organizational Scaling**

* Lead the operational framework for replication of the DT Community Model across new offices and client sites, ensuring standardized processes, staffing plans, and governance structures.
* Own and maintain the standardized enterprise core of DT's client site staff operating manuals — security, clinical operations, onboarding, and site procedures — into which each site integrates its approved protocol variations.
* Design and standardize core operational playbooks for repeatable agency processes such as new program launches, location openings, procurement cycles, IT deployments, and cross\-agency initiatives.
* Partner with the EDSS to operationalize scalable service delivery models while preserving program quality, compliance, and client experience during expansion.
* Manage corporate administrative facilities, leases, and enterprise physical infrastructure. (Housing program sites remain under Social Services leadership, with close coordination on enterprise standards, compliance, and facilities support.)
* Manage NYC and ATL office managers to promote consistent, professional, efficient office operations and employee and visitor experience.

**Risk, Compliance \& Legal** 

* Serve as primary Enterprise Risk Manager, owning the Enterprise Risk Register by consolidating risk inputs across the agency and external stakeholders, and maintaining visibility across financial, operational, programmatic, compliance, and reputational risks.
* Define and enforce a standardized enterprise risk rating methodology (e.g., likelihood × impact), ensuring consistency in reporting across all verticals.
* Establish and facilitate quarterly Enterprise Risk Reviews with executive leadership to evaluate risk exposure, mitigation strategies, and mitigation planning.
* Escalate material risks to the CEO and Board as appropriate, ensuring timely transparency and decision\-making.
* Oversee Compliance, ensuring regulatory adherence across all jurisdictions, including IT security, data privacy, facility safety, contract governance, and enterprise\-wide legal coordination.
* Oversee organizational insurance coverage, vendor risk assessments, procurement standards, and contract policies to ensure risk mitigation and operational consistency.

**Technology, Data \& Analytics**

* Oversee the enterprise IT strategy and manage the IT Lead, ensuring systems scalability, cybersecurity posture, CRM optimization, and technology alignment with organizational growth.
* Ensure analytics infrastructure supports enterprise governance needs, as well as program outcome measurement frameworks as defined by the EDSS and financial forecasting requirements defined by the CFO.
* Ensure data integrity, system alignment, and cross\-department reporting consistency across all locations and service lines.
* Manage the Data \& Analytics Lead, ensuring development and maintenance of agency\-wide dashboards, KPI tracking systems, and integrated reporting frameworks that support executive governance, risk review, financial forecasting, and program performance measurement.

**Workforce Infrastructure \& Organizational Capacity**

* Integrate workforce planning into operational scaling efforts, including new site launches, service expansions, and leadership successi

jobs.description:
**TADA! ASSOCIATE DIRECTOR OF INSTITUTIONAL GIVING**

**TADA! is a unique, Drama Desk award\-winning nonprofit youth theater that produces original musicals performed by youth for family audiences as well as a variety of Arts Education Programming. TADA! offers a free, year\-round, pre\-professional training and a positive youth development program for the Resident Youth Ensemble for ages 8–18; musical theater classes/camps for children 4–12; as well as musical theater residencies in NYC schools and community centers.**

**TADA! has an annual operating budget of almost $3 million, with goals to continue growing contributed revenue, particularly in individual giving and institutional support.**

**TADA! is seeking a full\-time Associate Director of Institutional Giving to work with the Development staff to raise contributed income that supports TADA! institutionally as well as all programming across education, the Resident Youth Ensemble (RYET), and theater departments.** 

**We’re looking for a passionate individual who believes in our mission and values (see below). You must be a friendly, joyous “people person” who likes to actually do the work as well as collaborate with staff and volunteers.** 

**TADA! Youth Theater is committed to building and fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce that reflects the young people we serve. Priority will be given to candidates dedicated to anti\-racism and aligned with our mission and vision. BIPOC candidates, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA\+ individuals are encouraged to apply.**

**Reports to: Executive and Producing Artistic Director** 

**Key Responsibilities:**

**With the Development and Leadership Team, significantly grow the organization’s contributed income and visibility through:**

* **Increased engagement and giving of current institutional (Foundations, Corporations and Government) donors**
* **Identifying, cultivating, soliciting, and managing new institutional donors**
* **Create and manage a strong strategic institutional fundraising plan that leverages Board members, staff, alumni, current families, audiences and volunteers**
* **Initiate and Manage relationships with corporate, foundation, and government funders; writing compelling proposals, applications and reports**
* **Partnering with Finance on grant budgets, reporting, audit, and filings**

**Qualifications:**

**The ideal candidate has:**

* **3\-5\+ years of nonprofit fundraising experience (preference in the arts), with demonstrated success in meeting or exceeding revenue goals for institutional support**
* **Experience working closely with a Board of Directors, and other volunteer groups such as a Junior Board and Parents**
* **Passion for and alignment with TADA!’s mission and communities served**
* **Commitment to equity, accessibility, inclusion, diversity and anti\-racism**
* **Strong strategic thinking, planning, and problem\-solving skills**
* **Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail**
* **Outstanding written and oral communication skills with diverse audiences, internally and externally**
* **Strong interpersonal skills and positive attitude and the ability to build rapport with diverse colleagues \& external stakeholders**
* **Experience with budget development**
* **Comfort with data systems; Salesforce experience a plus**
* **Ability to manage and prioritize multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment**
* **Confidence in donor cultivation and solicitation at all levels**
* **A hands\-on, high\-energy approach with both strategic and day\-to\-day execution**
* **Interest in musical theater and familiarity with the industry**

**Additional Information:**

**Hours:**Hybrid (4 days in the office). M\-F, 10AM\-6PM with some evening and weekend commitments (hours will vary depending on seasonal programming demands)

**Salary/Benefits:**$55,000 \- $65,000 Full\-time non\-exempt with benefits package: Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF)\-approved organization; Health, vision, dental, and life insurance; Pre\-tax: Flexible spending, transportation, Aflac, 403(b); 10 paid holidays, 5 sick days, 3 personal days, 10 paid personal vacation days, summer ½ day Fridays, week off between Christmas and New Year; week off end of August into Labor Day. If you have children that are in the age range of on\-site classes and camps \- free enrollment; free tickets to productions.

**TADA! is an Equal Opportunity Employer (EOE) and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, creed, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, class, physical ability, or veteran status.**

**We believe there are great candidates who may not meet every qualification but bring transferable skills and experience. We encourage you to apply and share why you are the right fit.**

**To apply, please email your cover letter and resume to jtrevens@tadatheater.com with “Associate Director of Institutional Giving” in the subject line. To learn more about TADA!, please visit our website: tadatheater.com**

Pay: $55,000\.00 \- $65,000\.00 per year

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Operating Officer**

Commerce CPA, LLC · New York, NY · Full\-Time Leadership Role

Commerce CPA is a fast\-growing CPA firm serving individuals and businesses across tax, bookkeeping, audit, payroll, and advisory. We are profitable, referral\-driven, and expanding quickly. To support the next stage of growth, we are hiring our first Chief Operating Officer to take full ownership of firm operations so the founder can focus exclusively on client development and growth.

**Why this role exists**

Our founder currently splits his time between two very different jobs: winning new business and running the entire operation. That split is now the single biggest constraint on our growth. Today the founder is pulled into day\-to\-day delivery — chasing client responses, confirming return statuses, reviewing work before it goes out, resolving software and workflow issues, and keeping deadlines from slipping. This role exists to change that permanently.

We are looking for a seasoned operations leader who has already done this at a larger firm and can step in to own every operational function end to end. The founder’s job is to bring in and sign clients. Your job is everything after that — delivering the work accurately, on time, and to a standard that keeps every client satisfied, while building the systems and team that let the firm scale without the founder’s involvement in operations.

**The mandate**

* **Take operations off the founder’s plate entirely.** Once a client is signed and engaged, you own the relationship’s delivery, quality, and outcome. The founder should not be the escalation path, the reviewer, or the person chasing status.
* **Scale revenue from \~$1M to $3–$5M within the next year** without a drop in quality, a loss of clients, or a capacity crisis. Build the operational engine that makes aggressive growth safe.
* **Protect and grow the client base.** Zero clients lost to responsiveness, missed deadlines, or avoidable errors. Client satisfaction is your primary scorecard.
* **Institutionalize how the firm runs.** Replace founder\-dependent, ad\-hoc processes with documented systems, clear ownership, and reliable execution across every function.

**What you will own**

Full operational accountability across all service lines and support functions:

**Service delivery — all functions**

* Tax (individual, business, multi\-state, crypto, and specialty filings), bookkeeping (monthly close, bank/feed reconciliations, financial statements), audit and attest engagements, payroll, and advisory — delivered accurately and on schedule.
* End\-to\-end workflow: engagement kickoff, document collection, preparation, review, filing/e\-file, and delivery to the client.
* A real quality\-control layer so nothing reaches a client without proper review — no missed filings, no unreviewed work, no re\-files.

**Client experience**

* Own client communication standards and response\-time SLAs across the whole team. Clients should never wait on a reply, and the founder should never have to prompt staff to respond.
* Complete visibility into every engagement’s status at any moment — what’s in progress, what’s pending, and what’s at risk — so nothing falls through the cracks.
* Manage escalations and turn service problems into retained, satisfied clients.

**Systems, process \& technology**

* Build, document, and enforce SOPs for every recurring process — onboarding, e\-file, follow\-ups, invoicing, client meetings, and file management — and keep them current.
* Own the tech stack and make it work as one system: TaxDome, the tax software (Drake / CCH), QuickBooks (Online and Desktop, including migrations), scheduling, and communication tools.
* Design workflows that are efficient and repeatable, drawing on what has worked at larger, more mature firms.

**Team leadership \& capacity**

* Lead, manage, and hold accountable the full delivery team (onshore and offshore) — tax, bookkeeping, audit, and administrative staff.
* Own capacity and busy\-season planning: staffing, workload balancing, and hiring so the firm can absorb rapid client growth without burnout or bottlenecks.
* Set performance expectations, run the daily/weekly operating cadence, and develop the team.

**Financial operations**

* Own invoicing, billing, and collections so revenue is captured and cash flows predictably.
* Track operational and delivery metrics, and report the health of the operation to the founder.

**What success looks like**

* **The founder is fully out of operations** — spending his time on sales and growth, not delivery or status\-chasing.
* **Growth target hit:** the firm scales toward $3–$5M with delivery quality intact.
* **Client retention and satisfaction rise;** churn from service issues goes to zero.
* **Deadlines are met reliably** with no missed or late filings.
* **The firm runs on documented systems** and a self\-sufficient team — not on the founder’s memory or intervention.

**First 12 months**

* **First 90 days:** learn the book of business and team, map every current process, and stand up a single source of truth for engagement status and client communication.
* **By 6 months:** documented SOPs live across all functions, a working quality\-review process, response\-time SLAs enforced, and the founder removed from day\-to\-day delivery.
* **By 12 months:** a scalable operating model and staffing plan proven to support $3–$5M, with clean handoffs from sales to delivery and no capacity crises.

**Who you are**

* **Proven COO / Director of Operations experience at a larger accounting or professional\-services firm** — you have run operations at scale and know what “good” looks like.
* **You have built and scaled operations before,** taking a firm through rapid growth while improving — not sacrificing — quality and client satisfaction.
* **Deep understanding of public\-accounting delivery:** tax, bookkeeping, audit, and the compliance calendar and deadlines that drive the work.

Description:

An exciting opportunity to join the team of a progressive YMCA located in vibrant Somerset County, New Jersey. The YMCA is one of the nation’s leading nonprofit organizations dedicated to strengthening communities through youth development, healthy living and social responsibility. With a 150\-year\-old history, Greater Somerset County YMCA (GSCY) is a pillar of the community and has seven locations across three counties, which collectively serve upwards of 30,000 individuals. GSCY has 100 full\-time and 950 part\-time employees along with operating revenues to $22\+ million.


The Y empowers everyone—no matter who they are or where they are from. To fulfill our purpose of strengthening community for all, GSCY is committed to being an inclusive organization and leveraging our collective impact to address social inequities. To learn more about GSCY, visit our website: http://gscymca.org

jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in working at William Paterson University.

**William Paterson University** is a purpose\-driven, proudly diverse community—designated as both a public Hispanic\-Serving Institution and Minority\-Serving Institution—committed to creating transformative academic, professional, and personal experiences as a powerhouse of progress. We foster a workplace that values growth, flexibility, and community, and we are recognized as a 2024 and 2025 *Forbes’ Best Employers by State, 2025 Great College to Work For,* and honored with the *NJBIZ Empowering Women Award*.

jobs.title: Learning Through Drama Program Director
jobs.description:
Thank you for considering a career with the Research Foundation of The City University of New York (RFCUNY).


The team at RFCUNY is made up of dedicated, talented professionals committed to providing the services that allow CUNY researchers, faculty, and staff to focus on their intellectual curiosity and scientific discoveries.


We are pleased that you are interested in exploring opportunities to join RFCUNY.


Primary Location:


OFFICE OF SR. UNIV DEAN FOR ACADEMIC AFFAIRS
Bargaining Unit:


No


The Creative Arts Team (CAT) is an arts\-in\-education program at the City University of New York (CUNY), serving more than 9,000 people annually in New York City. CAT uses theatre strategies to engage, inspire, and educate in a variety of settings, including schools, after\-school programs, college campuses, community\-based organizations, and government facilities.


CAT’s Learning Through Drama (LTD) team uses theme\-based, interactive drama and skill–building strategies to address social issues and behaviors that create challenges in everyday lives. The LTD team delivers programs to grades 6\-12, college students, and parents and adults. SEED programs address college readiness and retention, social\-emotional learning, conflict resolution, and skills for success in school and life. SEED programs provide a safe environment for participants to examine choices, decisions, and consequences. This process provides an opportunity to discover underlying motives, raise awareness, and provide solutions while promoting problem\-solving, decision\-making, critical thinking, and self\-advocacy skills to build confidence in achieving desired outcomes.


The Program Director reports to the Executive Director and provides strategic leadership, management, and oversight for a portfolio of programs. The Program Director develops curriculum; designs and delivers professional development training for teaching artists; and supervises project teams that may include full\-time staff, part\-time teaching artists, interns, and specialized consultants as needed. The position is responsible for understanding and ensuring compliance with all grants, contract, and award requirements, and for creating systems that support high\-quality program implementation in middle schools, high schools, and community settings. The Program Director oversees all aspects of program planning, implementation, staffing, partnerships, budgeting, evaluation, and reporting. The portfolio may include DYCD Crisis Management System and City Council\-funded initiatives, education programs, social\-emotional learning (SEL) professional development, grant\-funded workshops, and other strategic initiatives that advance the mission of the Creative Arts Team.

· Develop annual program structure and oversee implementation of all projects


· Directly supervise 6\-12 Actor Teachers, Associate Program director/s, and Program Manager/s

· Manage relationships with funders in collaboration with Development and Executive Director.


· Hire program staff, including organizing and hosting auditions and interviews as necessary, as well as onboarding paperwork in collaboration with colleagues at K\-16


· Train program staff in artistic and educational practices, including the pedagogical underpinnings of drama education and CAT work


· Interview, select, and orient MSW candidates to their work with CAT as part of the DYCD contract.

· Provide (or delegate to Associate Program Director/s) ongoing task supervision for MSW candidates throughout the year to ensure they are having a meaningful internship, providing valuable services to the school community, and have the support they need.


· Develop relationships with organizations that specialize in violence interruption to provide Safe Passages as required by DYCD.


· Provide (or delegate to Associate Program Director/s) ongoing supervision for Safe Passages providers throughout the year to ensure healthy relationships with school communities, effective programming, and added safety of students.


· Maintain and develop program vision, purpose, and direction, collaborating with program staff and Executive Director.


· Train program staff in administrative systems


· Structure the regular collaborative creation of drama\-education curriculum with Actor Teachers


* Work with Executive Director to create annual program budget at the start of the fiscal year and manage the budget throughout the year

· Work with Development (and external evaluators when applicable) to design and implement program evaluations, including but not limited to student reflection surveys, teacher reflection surveys, and CAT staff reflection.


· Collaborate with development and program staff to ensure grant compliance, overseeing systems for reporting, deadlines, and documentation.


· Review emails and respond in a timely manner to partners/staff/others.


· Report on program work, in writing, upon agreed timelines.


· Develop written materials for funders, partners, and others that represent the work of the program


· Facilitate the orientation of staff on CUNY/CAT protocols, including sick and annual leave, benefits, timesheets, office systems and norms, access to email and Drive, equipment use, fingerprinting, CUNY ID, etc.


· Comply with all RF CUNY Policies as documented on the RFCUNY website


· Comply with all CAT policies and contribute to development or revision of new CAT policies as needed.


· Maintain a respectful, professional relationship with fellow CAT and CUNY staff, including attendance of mandated CUNY and CAT trainings that help to support a respectful and healthy work environment.

jobs.title: Elder Justice Program Director (3yrs exp.)
jobs.description:
The Program Director of the Elder Justice Program is responsible for the overall leadership, administrative oversight, and supervision of the Elder Justice Program. This role ensures the delivery of high\-quality support services to clients and families served, compliance with all program standards, and adherence to contractual and regulatory requirements. The Program Director works closely with staff, community partners, funders, and agency leadership to support caregivers and strengthen the program’s impact.

**Organizational Values:** The Community Agency for Senior Citizens (CASC) assists Staten Islanders age 60 and older to live as independently as possible in the community. We do this by working in together with older adults, respecting their right to make decisions for themselves, honoring their life experiences and heritage, and recognizing the importance of friends, relatives and community in creating supportive environments for people as they age.

**Key Results:**

· Elder Justice Program operates effectively and efficiently

· Detailed program record keeping leads to timely submission of reports as required by NYC Aging and CASC

· Continuous Quality Improvement goals achieved

· Clients served receive high quality, individualized services and supports to meet their needs

· Effective implementation of NYC Aging and CASC procedures and guidelines

jobs.title: Deputy Director, Housing, Queens
jobs.description:
Legal Services NYC (LSNYC) fights poverty and seeks racial, social and economic justice for low\-income New Yorkers. For over 50 years, we have challenged systemic injustices that trap people in poverty and provided legal services that help our clients meet basic needs for housing, income and economic security, family and immigration stability, education, and health care. LSNYC is the largest civil legal services provider in the country; our staff of more than 600 people in neighborhood\-based offices and outreach sites across all five boroughs helps hundreds of thousands of New Yorkers annually. We partner with scores of community\-based and client\-run organizations, elected officials, public agencies, pro bono lawyers, and the courts to maximize our effectiveness. Our work fights discrimination and helps to achieve equity for all low\-income New Yorkers.



LSNYC employees have numerous opportunities for growth and professional development, including access to our internal Justice Learning Center, which provides opportunities to earn free CLEs and gain experience as a trainer.

**Deputy Director, Housing, Queens**



Founded in 1967, QLS seeks equal access to justice for all low\-income residents of Queens through a range of legal advocacy, education, and community partnerships. We provide free legal counseling, representation, and referrals in civil matters involving the essentials of life including housing, public and disability benefits, domestic violence prevention, consumer and homeowner protections, access to education, and employment rights to eligible low\-income individuals and families. We work with our clients and partners to identify and address root causes for systemic inequalities in Queens and throughout New York City. We provide civil legal services from our office in Jamaica, Queens.
We are part of a network of local programs that make up Legal Services NYC, the largest free civil legal services provider in the United States.

**Role \& Responsibilties:**

The Supervisor will join a vibrant practice in the heart of Southeast Queens, serving low\-income tenants throughout the borough. We are looking for a candidate who is excited to work in a fast\-paced environment on housing\-related matters, including landlord/tenant actions, eviction defense, and affirmative litigation on behalf of tenants. Responsibilities include directly supervising several housing attorneys and staff, representing clients in the full range of housing matters handled by the HRP, and supporting its day\-to\-day functioning.
The Supervisor will oversee a committed team of attorneys and staff representing low\-income and elderly Queens residents in housing matters and will be expected to work closely with tenant organizers, tenant associations, and community partners to identify and address housing needs. The attorney will work closely with the Housing Unit Director and other Supervisors to shape the direction of the HRP, develop and strengthen relationships with partner community\-based organizations handle administrative and grant responsibilities, and identify systemic housing\-related trends that could be affected by strategic litigation. The Supervisor will be expected to oversee and conduct weekly, evening, and weekend outreach and off\-site intake with our partners. S/he will also be expected to support litigation and advocacy on a range of issues, coordinate building\-wide and community\-based litigation, and organize community education outreach and related initiatives.
The attorney will supervise regular appearances in civil, supreme, and federal court and administrative proceedings, review draft pleadings, motions, and court submissions, and support the filing of affirmative cases on behalf of tenants and tenant groups.
  

**Experience \& Qualifications:**  

  

We seek an attorney admitted to the New York State Bar with:
* A demonstrated passion for social justice and commitment to working with immigrant communities, communities of color, and low\-income communities to advance equity;
* Excellent analytical, research, and writing skills;
* Demonstrated experience with housing advocacy;
* Demonstrated experience supervising legal work (preferred);
* Strong interpersonal, communication, time management, and organizational skills;
* Enthusiasm for and experience developing partnerships with community \-based advocates and organizations;
* A facility working with software technology and proficiency in the entire MS Office suite;
* Experience with LegalServer or other case management systems (preferred); and
* Fluency or strong proficiency in Spanish, Mandarin, Cantonese, Bengali, or Korean (preferred).
* 2 or more years supervisory experience strongly preferred
* Familiarity with LSC rules and regulations (preferred)

  

**How to Apply**  

  

Interested candidates should email a cover letter, resume, two recent writing samples, and the names of three references below. Only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted. No telephone calls, please.  

All employees are strongly encouraged to continue to receive and maintain up\-to\-date COVID vaccinations unless they are unable to for medical or religious reasons.



Legal Services NYC is an equal opportunity employer. People of color, women, people with disabilities, people over 40, and gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender people are strongly encouraged to apply.

jobs.title: Senior Director of Advancement
jobs.description:
**About the Organization:**

Family Nurturing Center of Massachusetts (FNC) was founded in 1994 with the mission to advance the healthy development of children, caregivers, and communities through parenting education, early childhood engagement, coalition building, and training for providers. For more than 30 years, FNC has provided free family engagement programs in Boston's underserved neighborhoods, with recent expansion to Chelsea, MA, and offered nationally recognized training and professional development services.

Each year, FNC engages over 8,500 children, caregivers, and professionals. Programs are offered in multiple languages and are grounded in values of equity, collaboration, and family leadership.

In 2020, FNC opened a 12,800\-square\-foot headquarters in Dorchester, expanding its capacity to serve families and strengthen community partnerships. With a staff of 72 (full and part\-time), a committed cross\-sector board of directors, and an annual operating budget of $6M, FNC is poised for continued impact.

jobs.description:
**Job Overview**

The Director of Development for Habitat for Humanity of Southwest Utah leads and oversees all fundraising and resource development efforts to support the organization's mission and expand its homebuilding capacity. Reporting to the Executive Director, this role develops and manages annual giving campaigns, major donor and corporate sponsorship programs, grant writing and management, planned giving initiatives, and fundraising events. The position also cultivates relationships with donors, volunteers, community organizations, and board members while assisting with strategic planning, budgeting, marketing, public relations, and community outreach. Success in this role requires strong communication, relationship\-building, and organizational skills, along with proven experience in nonprofit fundraising, grant development, and donor stewardship.

**Responsibilities**: The Director of Development plans, manages and implements annual fundraising activities and assists the Executive Director in general administration, financial management, community relations and strategic planning. Specific duties include but are not limited to:

**Capital giving for homebuilding and annual fund**

· Develop and implement donor cultivation and solicitation program

· Develop and sustain productive relationships with major gift constituencies, including business and corporate sponsors and individual donors

· Direct and support the fundraising efforts of the Board, volunteers, and staff

· Develop and implement annual giving programs, including but not limited to direct mail, matching gifts, monthly giving, and online giving

· Assist with the management and effective use of the Habitat’s database relating to donors, donor recognition and fundraising activity

· Write, update and evaluate the annual fund development plan

**Grant management**

· Research grant funding opportunities to support annual operating fund and capital giving for homebuilding needs

· Maintain annual calendar of grant activity and deadlines

· Write and submit proposals for funding and prepare reports and other documents related to grant activity

**Special events**

· Direct planning and activities to support the annual signature event with fundraiser committee volunteers

· Work with fundraiser committee volunteers to sustain and build signature event success

· Access community groups and businesses engaged in charitable giving opportunities through fundraisers

· Assist staff with annual meeting and volunteer recognition event planning and implementation

**Planned Giving**

· Work with the Board, Executive Director, and staff, to establish a planned giving program

· Integrate planned giving program with annual fund donor cultivation and solicitation efforts

**Additional Responsibilities:**

· Works with the Executive Director to prepare an annual budget for resource development activities that include fundraising, public relations, and marketing

· Serves as staff liaison to the Fundraising Committee

· Coordinates quarterly newsletter content with HFH Staff

· Seek out and schedule opportunities to make informational presentations to churches, business, civic organizations and other community groups

· Develop relationships with the local nonprofit and philanthropic community for the purpose of informational exchange and support

**Qualifications**: Bachelor’s degree and a minimum three years successful experience in fundraising.

**Requirements/Skills**: Must be goal\-driven, results\-oriented and committed to the organization’s effort to increase its homebuilding capacity. Must have an understanding of the mission, history, values and goals of our Non\-profit organization and be able to communicate this information to diverse groups of potential donors and volunteers. Must demonstrate strong written, oral and interpersonal communication skills. The ability to work effectively as a team member with staff and volunteers is essential. Ability to work with and facilitate gifts with major donors, ability to manage and sustain an annual fund for a nonprofit organization and experience in planning and organizing major fund\-raising events necessary.

Join us as a **Director of Development** for a non\-profit where your energy fuels growth and change in our own community! Bring your expertise in fundraising, relationship management, and strategic planning to make a meaningful impact while working with a passionate team committed to excellence. We provide an inclusive environment that values innovation, collaboration, and your professional growth—empowering you every step of the way!

Pay: $55,000\.00 \- $60,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

People with a criminal record are encouraged to apply

Work Location: In person

jobs.description:
**NeuroPong Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator**

**Position Overview**

NeuroPong is seeking an energetic, highly organized, dependable, and mission\-driven *‘Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator’* to work directly with our Founder and CEO, Dr. Antonio Barbera, in supporting the day\-to\-day operations of our rapidly growing nonprofit organization.

This is a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in nonprofit leadership, healthcare innovation, community engagement, event management, and program development while helping improve the lives of individuals living with Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, dementia, stroke, traumatic brain injury, and other neurological conditions through the transformative power of table tennis.

The *Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator* will play an important role in helping NeuroPong (http://neuropong.org) expand its programs, strengthen community partnerships, coordinate events, and support organizational growth. No two days are the same, making this an ideal position for someone who enjoys variety, takes initiative, and thrives in a dynamic, purpose\-driven environment.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Provide administrative and organizational support to the Founder and CEO in the day\-to\-day operations of NeuroPong.
* Assist with scheduling, communications, meeting coordination, correspondence, travel arrangements, and other administrative tasks.
* Help maintain participant, volunteer, event, and organizational records through data entry and use of online databases and software systems.
* Provide support for NeuroPong programs, events, fundraising activities, marketing initiatives, community outreach, and special projects as needed.
* Assist with event preparation, logistics, registration, equipment, and follow\-up for classes, demonstrations, conferences, and community events.
* Help monitor ongoing projects, organize information, and ensure timely follow\-up on action items.
* Perform other administrative and operational duties as assigned in support of NeuroPong’s mission.

**What We Are Looking For**

* Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Dependable, self\-motivated, and able to work independently while managing multiple priorities
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and common online software platforms; willingness to learn new systems.
* Comfortable with data entry and maintaining accurate records.
* Interest in nonprofit organizations, healthcare, community service, or program management.
* Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation for occasional local meetings and events.
* Ability to occasionally lift and carry up to 50 pounds and assist with event setup.
* Willingness to work occasional evenings or weekends for special events.
* A genuine enthusiasm for NeuroPong’s mission and a desire to make a positive impact.

**Time Commitment**

* Part\-time position (approximately 10 hours per week).
* Flexible schedule.
* Hybrid work environment with a combination of remote work and in\-person meetings with the NeuroPong founder in Fort Collins, CO.
* Opportunity for increased responsibilities and additional hours as NeuroPong continues to grow.

**Compensation**

**$25\.00 per hour**

**Why Join NeuroPong?**

This is an opportunity to become part of an organization that is redefining neurorehabilitation through movement, joy, and human connection.

As the *Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator,* you will work closely with the Founder and CEO, gaining firsthand experience in nonprofit leadership, healthcare innovation, strategic planning, community outreach, fundraising, program development, marketing, and organizational growth. You'll build meaningful relationships with healthcare professionals, researchers, community leaders, and individuals living with neurological conditions while contributing to a mission that is improving lives around the world.

If you're looking for a position where your work truly makes a difference, where you'll develop valuable professional skills, and where you'll have the opportunity to grow alongside an innovative international nonprofit, we'd love to hear from you.

**Contact**

Antonio Barbera, NeuroPong Founder and CEO, info@neuropong,org

Pay: $25\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Fort Collins, CO 80524

jobs.description:
**Assistant Director of Grants and Compliance**

**Reports to:** Director of Development

 **Functional Area:** Advancement Department

 **FLSA Status:** Exempt

 **Job Location:** Ward 5

 **Classification:** Manager

 **Direct Reports:** 1 (Grants Systems Specialist)

  




**POSITION OVERVIEW**


Academy of Hope Adult Public Charter School (AoH)'s Advancement Team seeks an Assistant Director of Grants and Compliance to lead the organization's grants portfolio and grants team. Established in 1985, AoH provides education, workforce development training, and college and job placement to adults in DC. Our mission is to support adult learners in changing their lives and transforming their communities by providing high\-quality adult education and career training that create direct pathways to economic mobility.


The Assistant Director of Grants and Compliance leads AoH's grants program from end to end: identifying funding opportunities and developing competitive proposals to secure government, corporate, and foundation grants; managing the full grant lifecycle from award through close\-out; and ensuring AoH's grants are administered in compliance with funder requirements and applicable federal, state, and local regulations. This role directly supervises the Grants Systems Specialist and is accountable for the performance, development, and workflow of the grants team. This position works closely with the Finance team on budget development, grant compliance, and financial reporting, and partners with Academic Leaders, Program Managers, and the Performance Evaluation \& Research team to align grant deliverables with program realities. This position reports to the Director of Development and works closely with the Chief Strategy \& Growth Officer.

**A. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

**GRANT SEEKING \& INSTITUTIONAL GIVING**

* Manage, grow, and optimize a robust foundation, government, and corporate giving program in collaboration with the Director of Development and Chief Strategy \& Growth Officer, implementing cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship strategies.
* Build and manage a foundation and government funding portfolio, meeting institutional giving revenue goals set by the Director of Development.
* Identify prospective funders, research funding requirements, and maintain an up\-to\-date pipeline of potential funders aligned with AoH's mission and program priorities.
* Oversee the development and submission of letters of inquiry, proposals, and grant applications, writing and editing proposals in collaboration with program staff and leadership.
* Build relationships with regional and national institutional partners; identify and attend funder\-sponsored events to build relationships and raise AoH's visibility.

**GRANTS MANAGEMENT \& COMPLIANCE**

* Own the full grant lifecycle — pre\-award, award, implementation, reporting, and close\-out — for all corporate, foundation, and government grants.
* Ensure grant administration complies with funder terms and applicable regulations, including federal Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200\) for federally funded and federally passed\-through awards, and DC government grant requirements where applicable.
* Review grant agreements and contract terms prior to execution, flagging compliance risks, reporting obligations, and budget restrictions for leadership.
* Partner with the Finance team to develop, monitor, and reconcile grant budgets, ensuring allowable\-cost compliance and timely resolution of variances.
* Monitor all grant deadlines in AoH's CRM for deliverables, reports, and renewals, ensuring timely and accurate submission across all funding sources.
* Maintain the CRM and grant files as the system of record for proposals, awards, budgets, and correspondence; regularly report grant metrics and compliance status to AoH leadership.
* Support audit readiness by maintaining organized grant records, supporting documentation, and expenditure tracking in accordance with AoH's record\-retention policy.
* Coordinate with Academic Leaders, Program Managers, and the Performance Evaluation \& Research team to monitor grant\-funded project goals, deliverables, and budgets throughout the grant period.

**TEAM LEADERSHIP \& SUPERVISION**

* Directly supervise the Grants Systems Specialist, including hiring, onboarding, training, delegating workflow, and conducting regular one\-on\-ones.
* Set annual goals and conduct formal performance reviews for direct report(s); provide ongoing coaching and identify professional development opportunities.
* Build and document grants\-management systems and procedures so grant knowledge and compliance practices are not dependent on a single person.
* Foster a collaborative, accountable team culture and ensure adequate coverage of grant deadlines during staff absences or transitions.

**DEVELOPMENT OPERATIONS**

* Provide monthly reports to the Director of Development on institutional giving and grants\-compliance progress against goals.
* Partner with the Director of Development to update policies, processes, and procedures for Development operations, including foundation and grants\-management policies.
* Support engagement of the CEO, Chief Strategy \& Growth Officer, and other senior staff with institutional funders, including preparing annual pipelines, briefings ahead of visits, and appropriate follow\-up.
* Assist with setting annual and multi\-year goals for corporate, foundation, and government funder relations in consultation with the Advancement Team and departmental leadership.

**QUALIFICATIONS**

* Bachelor's degree and at least five years of progressively responsible experience in institutional giving and/or grants management, including a track record of setting and meeting revenue and compliance goals.
* At least two years of experience directly supervising staff, including perf

jobs.title: Director of Institutional Giving
jobs.description:
**Location:** Miami, FL (Headquarters)  

**Schedule:** Full\-Time


**FLSA Status:** Exempt  

**Salary:** $85,000–$105,000 annually

 **About Big Brothers Big Sisters of Miami**



For more than 65 years, Big Brothers Big Sisters of Miami has been creating life\-changing mentoring relationships that empower young people to reach their full potential. Through the power of one\-to\-one mentorship, we help children build confidence, achieve greater educational success, and realize brighter futures.

  

We are seeking a strategic and relationship\-driven **Director of Institutional Giving** to lead and grow our foundation, corporate, and government fundraising efforts. This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced development professional to play a key leadership role in advancing the mission of one of South Florida's most respected nonprofit organizations.

 **Position Summary**



Reporting to the **Vice President of Development**, the Director of Institutional Giving is responsible for developing and executing strategies that increase and diversify institutional funding. The successful candidate will manage a portfolio of foundation, corporate, and government funders while identifying new funding opportunities that support BBBS Miami's strategic priorities.

  

This position manages an annual institutional fundraising portfolio of approximately **$3–4 million** and works closely with executive leadership, program staff, and the Board of Directors to secure transformational investments that expand the organization's impact throughout the community.

 **Primary Responsibilities**


* Develop and implement a comprehensive institutional giving strategy aligned with organizational goals.
* Build, manage, and grow a portfolio of foundation, corporate, and government funding partners.
* Identify, research, cultivate, solicit, and steward prospective and current institutional donors.
* Monitor philanthropic trends and identify emerging funding opportunities at the local, state, and national levels.
* Lead the development of compelling grant proposals, letters of inquiry, reports, and other institutional fundraising materials.
* Collaborate with program and executive leadership to develop program budgets, funding requests, and organizational case statements.
* Ensure timely submission of grant applications, reports, and all funder deliverables.
* Oversee stewardship activities and donor recognition for institutional supporters.
* Partner with the Vice President of Development to establish fundraising goals and monitor progress throughout the year.
* Collaborate with the CEO, senior leadership team, Board members, and volunteers to cultivate and strengthen institutional relationships.
* Maintain accurate donor records and fundraising activity within Raiser’s Edge (or similar CRM).
* Prepare fundraising reports, pipeline updates, and progress reports for leadership and the Board of Directors.
* Support annual audit activities and other organizational reporting requirements as needed.

  


**Qualifications**



The ideal candidate is a strategic fundraiser, exceptional writer, and collaborative relationship builder who thrives in a mission\-driven environment.


* Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional fundraising experience.
* Five or more years of progressively responsible fundraising experience, with a demonstrated record of securing institutional funding.
* Experience managing foundation, corporate, and/or government grants from cultivation through stewardship.
* Strong grant writing, proposal development, editing, and presentation skills.
* Experience developing program budgets and funding proposals.
* Knowledge of local, regional, and national philanthropic communities and institutional funding sources.
* Experience using donor CRM systems such as Raiser’s Edge or similar fundraising databases.
* Excellent organizational, project management, and time management skills.
* Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships across diverse stakeholders.
* High level of professionalism, discretion, and sound judgment.
* CFRE certification is a plus.

  


**Why Join BBBS of Miami**



Joining Big Brothers Big Sisters of Miami means becoming part of a mission\-driven organization committed to changing the trajectory of young people's lives through mentoring.

  

As a member of our Development team, you will have the opportunity to shape fundraising strategy, build meaningful partnerships with institutional supporters, and directly influence the growth and sustainability of programs that serve youth throughout Miami\-Dade County.

  

We offer a collaborative work environment, opportunities for professional growth, and a comprehensive benefits package for full\-time employees.

 **Work Environment \& Schedule**



This is a hybridposition (4 in office, 1 WFH) based at BBBS of Miami's headquarters: **550 NW 42nd AvenueMiami, FL 33126\.** Occasional evening and weekend attendance at donor events, meetings, and organizational functions is expected.

 **Equal Employment Opportunity**



Big Brothers Big Sisters of Miami is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply.

  

If you're excited about this opportunity but don't meet every qualification, we encourage you to submit an application. We recognize that valuable experience comes in many forms and are committed to considering candidates with diverse backgrounds and perspectives.

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
Northfield Mount Hermon **(NMH)** seeks a strategic and collaborative Director of Development. Reporting to the Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) as their primary strategic partner, the Director of Development (DOD) functions as the department's operational leader, ensuring management continuity and alignment across all advancement initiatives. The position oversees all direct fundraising units including the NMH Fund, parent giving, major gifts for domestic and international supporters, and legacy giving with responsibility for securing financial success of Northfield Mount Hermon. The position manages a team of staff professionals and will work with many volunteers including Alumni and Family Councils, and others as needed both to accomplish the work and to engage constituents in meaningful ways. The position carries an advancement portfolio of 75\+ potential donors to meet institutional goals. **Why NMH**

* Comprehensive insurances, including medical, dental, vision, life, and disability
* A robust 11% 403(b) contribution (with a 3% employee contribution)
* Generous paid time off, including four weeks of annual paid vacation
* Tired of packing lunch? Enjoy meals in our dining hall while on duty and save an average of $2,700 a year.
* Tuition remission for dependent children of high school age who meet the acceptance requirements of the school

**About NMH**


Northfield Mount Hermon, an independent boarding and day school located in the beautiful Connecticut River Valley of Massachusetts, was founded on the principles of inclusivity and community — values that have been central to the school’s mission since 1879\. NMH engages the intellect, compassion, and talents of its students, empowering them to act with humanity and purpose.

**A Day in the Life**


The Director of Development will serve as a strategic partner to the Chief Advancement Officer and a key leader within NMH’s Advancement team. This position will oversee frontline fundraising efforts across annual giving, parent giving, major gifts, and legacy giving while leading a team of advancement professionals and collaborating with a dedicated network of volunteers. In addition to providing strategic leadership for NMH’s comprehensive development program, the Director of Development will cultivate relationships with alumni, parents, and friends through a personal portfolio of major and leadership\-level donors. This role will help shape fundraising strategies, strengthen donor engagement, and build systems that support the continued growth of NMH’s philanthropic community. The successful candidate will be an active member of the NMH community, building relationships across campus and developing a deep understanding of the school’s mission, traditions, and culture.

**Required Skills and Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree required
* 10\+ years of experience leading successful fundraising programs, including experience managing and developing high\-performing teams
* Demonstrated success developing fundraising strategies, cultivating donor relationships, and securing philanthropic support
* Experience managing and engaging volunteers in support of institutional goals
* Strong leadership, project management, and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to build authentic relationships with a wide range of constituents
* Ability to collaborate effectively across departments while working independently and exercising sound judgment
* Experience using technology platforms and tools, including Google Workspace or Microsoft Office
* Demonstrated discretion, tact, and ability to manage confidential information
* Commitment to working with a diverse and inclusive community and to NMH’s mission and values

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in educational fundraising, particularly within an independent school, college, or university setting
* Advanced degree preferred
* Experience working within a boarding school or residential educational community
* Familiarity with CRM systems such as Slate or other advancement databases


This is a full\-time, on\-site position based at NMH’s campus in Gill, Massachusetts. Frequent travel, along with evening and weekend availability, will be essential for success in this role.

We are seeking an experienced, strategic, and growth\-minded Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead a comprehensive behavioral health and dual diagnosis treatment network in Missouri. This executive will oversee daily operations across residential, detox, intensive outpatient (IOP), and outpatient (OP) programs. The ideal candidate will optimize clinical and administrative workflows, maintain strict regulatory compliance, and scale operations while upholding the highest standards of patient care.

jobs.description:
**Chief Executive Officer**


Title: Chief Executive Officer


Stage: Pre\-Seed Series A Healthcare Technology Company


Reports to: Board of Chief Executive Officer

**Remote (U.S.) \| Frequent Travel to San Francisco**

**About Bedrock Talent**
------------------------


At Bedrock Talent, we're intentional about the companies we represent.


We don't partner with every startup that comes our way. We work with founders we'd be excited to work for ourselves, companies solving meaningful problems, and teams we genuinely believe have the potential to build category\-defining businesses.


This is one of those companies.

**About the Company**
---------------------


Our client is an early\-stage healthcare AI company with an ambitious mission: leveraging artificial intelligence, data science, and clinical expertise to improve health outcomes and meaningfully extend healthy human lives.


Backed by an exceptional founding team and world\-class advisors spanning healthcare, technology, AI, and longevity science, the company is building a platform designed to transform complex health data into actionable insights for patients, providers, and researchers.


They're still in the earliest stages of company building, which means this role offers a rare opportunity to shape not only the business, but the future direction of an emerging category.

**The Opportunity**
-------------------


We're looking for a **Chief Executive Officer** who wants to build more than a company—they want to build a legacy.


This is a true 0 1 leadership opportunity for an operator who thrives at the intersection of product, commercialization, fundraising, and company building.


You'll partner with an experienced executive team and renowned advisors to define product\-market fit, establish go\-to\-market strategy, raise capital, recruit exceptional talent, and guide the company from its earliest stages through Series A and beyond.


This role is ideal for someone who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, making decisions with imperfect information, and building organizations from first principles.

**Before You Apply**
--------------------


Rather than bury the important details in a long job description, here are the things that usually determine whether someone gets excited about this opportunity—or realizes it probably isn't the right fit.

### **This Is a True 0 1 CEO Role**


The company has exceptional technical and scientific leadership already in place.


What it needs is a CEO who can connect product to market, vision to execution, and innovation to commercial success.


You'll help define nearly every aspect of how the company grows—from fundraising and hiring to customer acquisition and product strategy.

### **You'll Be the Face of the Company**


You'll serve as the primary storyteller for customers, investors, strategic partners, and future employees.


Success in this role requires someone equally comfortable pitching venture capitalists, meeting with healthcare executives, and recruiting world\-class talent.

### **Product and Go\-to\-Market Are Equally Important**


This isn't a "fundraising CEO" or a "sales CEO."


The ideal leader understands customers deeply, helps shape product strategy, builds repeatable go\-to\-market motions, and translates technical innovation into real customer value.

### **Mission Comes First**


The company exists to tackle one of humanity's biggest challenges: helping people live healthier, longer lives.


The team is looking for someone who believes meaningful businesses can generate exceptional returns while creating measurable improvements in people's lives.

### **Exceptional Founders and Advisors**


One of the reasons Bedrock Talent chose to partner with this company is the people behind it.


The founders and advisors are thoughtful, ambitious, low ego, and deeply committed to building the right company—not simply the fastest\-growing one.


They're looking for a CEO who shares those values.

**What You'll Do**
------------------

* Define and execute the company's product\-market fit strategy.
* Lead commercialization efforts, including early customer acquisition and strategic partnerships.
* Develop and own the company's go\-to\-market strategy, positioning, and pricing.
* Lead fundraising efforts from pre\-seed through Series A.
* Recruit and build a world\-class leadership team across product, engineering, commercial, and operations.
* Partner closely with technical leadership to ensure product development aligns with customer needs and market demand.
* Represent the company with customers, investors, partners, and the broader healthcare and AI ecosystem.
* Build a mission\-driven culture rooted in ownership, collaboration, and execution.

**We're Looking For Someone Who...**
------------------------------------

* Has successfully built and scaled an early\-stage technology company.
* Has led or played a significant role in venture fundraising.
* Has a proven track record of commercializing innovative products and driving revenue growth.
* Understands healthcare, health technology, or adjacent regulated industries.
* Can engage confidently with engineers, AI researchers, clinicians, and investors alike.
* Has repeatedly attracted exceptional talent into ambitious startup environments.
* Leads with humility, curiosity, and a strong bias toward execution.

**This Role Is Probably a Great Fit If...**
-------------------------------------------

* You've always wanted to build a company from the earliest stages.
* You're equally energized by fundraising, product strategy, customer conversations, and company building.
* You enjoy operating in ambiguity and creating structure as you scale.
* You believe the best CEOs spend as much time listening as they do leading.
* You're motivated by solving meaningful healthcare problems with lasting societal impact.
