Position Highlights The University of Arizona seeks a strategic and experienced leader to serve as its Executive Director of the Academic Financial Office. In this pivotal role, you work in close collaboration with the Chief Financial Officer, the Chief Budget \& Fiscal Management Officer, the Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO), the Provost, and the Deans, providing financial oversight for all 20 colleges of the University. Your responsibilities directly support the University's core academic mission, initiatives focused on student success, academic affairs, faculty and staff excellence, research, and key strategic objectives.
  
  

Reporting to the Chief Financial Officer (CFO), you will be a vital member of both the CFO and the Provost's administrative leadership teams. Your expertise will be instrumental in developing and implementing financial strategies for the near and long\-term, directly contributing to the University's overall academic and strategic achievements. Success in this role requires a comprehensive understanding of finance, outstanding management capabilities, and a versatile professional background relevant to the nuanced financial landscape of an R1 university's central academic administration.
  
  

As the Executive Director of the Academic Financial Office, you will have significant autonomy in designing and implementing a new model for financial operations of the colleges, which will be aligned with the University’s new budget model (Arizona Forward), eventually building and leading a team of finance professionals. You will foster a collaborative and customer\-focused environment while ensuring financial goals and daily operations are aligned with CFO and Provost's vision for academic excellence and student success.
  
  

Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.
  
  

Outstanding U of A benefits include health, dental, and vision insurance plans; life insurance and disability programs; paid vacation, sick leave, and holidays; U of A/ASU/NAU tuition reduction for the employee and qualified family members; retirement plans; access to U of A recreation and cultural activities; and more!
  
  

The University of Arizona has been recognized for our innovative work\-life programs.
  
  

Duties \& Responsibilities
  
  

**Academic Financial Team Leadership:**  

Lead the transition from a distributed academic financial model to a new structure that is aligned with the university’s financial structures and strategic imperatives and is managed in the CFO’s office in collaboration with academic leadership.
  
Exercise direct, solid\-line supervisory authority over and performance management of approximately 20 leaders/managers, fostering a culture of accountability, professional growth, and "whole\-university" thinking that transcends individual unit interests.
  
Lead and inspire a distributed workforce of over 200 business and finance professionals by establishing clear standards for operational excellence, transparency, and ethical stewardship across all academic units.
  
Set the cultural tone, model the collaborative behavior needed, and align the technical functions of academic business administration with the university's academic and research mission, ensuring all team members understand how their fiscal roles directly support faculty and staff success and student outcomes.
  
Drive large\-scale cultural change by implementing consistent, equitable practices in hiring, workload distribution, and professional development across the academic portfolio, reducing "silo" behaviors and promoting cross\-unit collaboration.
  
With the support of the Office of Business Affairs and with University Communications, design and execute a comprehensive internal communication strategy \- including "all\-hands" interactions, functional working groups, and digital tool sets \- to ensure all members of the team remain informed, engaged, and aligned with the CFO and Provost's strategic priorities.
  
Continuously assess and adjust leadership roles and team structures to ensure optimal efficiency and effectiveness in achieving the financial and University\-wide goals.
  
  

**Strategic Financial Planning \& Execution:**  

Lead the development of a comprehensive suite of multi\-year financial models for the colleges supporting initiatives related to areas such as academic programs, faculty development, student success, optimization of academic revenue streams and the fiscal impact of enrollment fluctuations. Take a lead role in the change management necessary for success.
  
Partner with the Provost, CHRO, and Deans to model and manage the long\-term fiscal impact of faculty affairs, including the competitive structuring of start\-up packages, retention funding, tenure\-track line allocations, and instructional capacity planning across all 20 colleges.
  
Oversee the execution of innovative operational plans and systems for integrated financial operations and budgeting processes strategically aligning the administration of academics with the University's strategic goals.
  
Facilitate cross\-functional collaboration across the colleges and with other central administrative units to integrate financial efforts related to the CFO's responsibilities.
  
Proactively identify and fix the root causes of financial challenges and opportunities related to academic strategic priorities.
  
  

**Financial Evaluation \& Business Planning:**  

Oversee the preparation of business and operational plans, ensuring a comprehensive evaluation of financial viability and strategic alignment.
  
Develop a standardized process for and conduct comprehensive financial due diligence for new programs and initiatives, ensuring a thorough examination of financial considerations.
  
Drive continuous improvement initiatives in financia

jobs.description:
**Assistant Director of Individual Giving**

**Reports to:** Director of Development

 **Functional Area:** Advancement Department

 **FLSA Status:** Exempt

 **Job Location:** Ward 5

 **Employment Type:** Full\-Time

 **Classification:** Manager

**POSITION OVERVIEW**


The Assistant Director of Individual Giving plays a central role in growing and diversifying Academy of Hope's (AoH) revenue base by building a strong, sustainable individual giving program. This role is especially important as AoH strengthens the day\-to\-day systems that support consistent donor engagement and stewardship.


Reporting to the Director of Development, the Assistant Director of Individual Giving will develop and implement AoH's individual giving strategy and annual plan — in partnership with, and subject to approval by, the Director of Development — manage performance against goals, and maintain a disciplined moves\-management and stewardship cadence in Salesforce CRM.


The Assistant Director will directly supervise and coach the Individual Giving Specialist, oversee the Annual Fund strategy and results, and oversee donor engagement event logistics.


This role also partners closely with the Director of Development and Chief Growth Officer — providing donor data, portfolio analysis, and prospect research that inform prioritization decisions and help prepare AoH leadership for high\-impact donor engagements. The ideal candidate brings an entrepreneurial mindset: someone who can build systems and processes that don't yet exist, test and refine approaches, and help AoH grow individual giving in a way that is measurable, repeatable, and sustainable.

**A. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Individual Giving Strategy, Pipeline Growth \& Moves Management** *(35%)*

* Establish and maintain a practical moves\-management cadence (e.g., weekly next\-step review, monthly portfolio refresh, quarterly pipeline health check) that drives measurable movement and reduces stalled relationships.
* Partner with the Director of Development to identify, qualify, and cultivate high\-capacity prospects, and support portfolio strategy and donor\-movement decisions.
* Track performance and pipeline health using Salesforce dashboards and regular reporting (pipeline value, donor movement, retention, attrition, upgrades, solicitations, and outcomes by segment).
* Maintain consistent processes and data standards in Salesforce so individual giving data is reliable for forecasting, decision\-making, and leadership reporting.
* Manage a personal portfolio of donors and prospects with giving potential up to $25,000, using clear moves management (stages, next steps, timelines) to advance relationships over time.
* Drive AoH's leadership annual giving program (the tier just below major gifts), including segmentation, cultivation, stewardship, and annual targets, in coordination with the Director of Development.
* Participate directly in solicitations for the leadership annual giving and mid\-level tiers, including strategy prep, donor meetings, proposal\-drafting support, follow\-up, and documenting outcomes in Salesforce within agreed\-upon timelines.

**Mid\-Level Giving \& Donor Relationship Management** *(20%)*

* Oversee a portfolio of mid\-level donors ($1,000–$9,999\), partnering with the Individual Giving Specialist on day\-to\-day cultivation and stewardship execution while retaining accountability for strategy, segmentation, and upgrade identification.
* Support the Director of Development in preparing senior leadership and board members for donor engagement and solicitation by developing donor and prospect briefing materials tailored to the meeting purpose (cultivation, stewardship, renewal, upgrade, or ask).
* Build and maintain meeting\-prep toolkits for donor visits (contact history, giving/engagement summary, affinity and interests, proposed talking points, "the ask," roles in the meeting, and next steps).
* In partnership with the Director of Development, develop board toolkits to strengthen annual fund execution and donor stewardship (call scripts, email templates, segmented call lists, talking points, and tracking instructions).
* Ensure donor experiences are consistent, personal, and aligned with AoH's mission and culture.

**Annual Fund Oversight** *(20%)*

* Oversee and manage the Annual Fund program, including strategy, calendar, segmentation, and performance tracking across direct mail, email, digital, and stewardship.
* Ensure campaign execution is consistent and measurable (e.g., Giving Tuesday, end\-of\-year, back\-to\-school, and spring campaign).
* Draft and coordinate annual fund messaging and stewardship communications to ensure alignment, tone, and outcomes\-based storytelling, with final approval from the Director of Development.

**Donor Engagement Events \& Cultivation Moments** *(10%)*

* Oversee donor engagement opportunities that strengthen relationships and move donors toward deeper investment (small cultivation gatherings, site visits, graduation touchpoints, and tailored experiences).
* Partner with internal teams to build authentic engagement moments tied to AoH programs and strategic priorities.
* Ensure every event has a defined fundraising purpose (stewardship, upgrade, prospecting, or cultivation) and a clear follow\-up plan in Salesforce (attendance logged, portfolio routed, next step set within a defined timeframe).

jobs.title: Program Director Youth Development - Perry
jobs.description:
YMCA of Greater Houston Youth Development Director supports the work of the Y, a leading nonprofit committed to strengthening community through youth development, healthy living, and social responsibility. This position develops, organizes and implements high\-quality YMCA afterschool and day camp throughout the Greater Houston area. The Youth Development Director will create a safe and positive atmosphere that welcomes and respects all individuals while inspiring youth to discover their passions. This position will enthusiastically represent the Y by displaying a positive attitude, maintaining a high level of energy and commitment to quality throughout all aspects of the job.

  



YMCA Youth Development Directors build and maintain strong relationships with families, community, and YMCA centers through positive proactive communication. They implement curriculum to meet licensing standards and improve quality through program evaluation and assessment. Youth Development Directors recruit, hire, and onboard YMCA site leads and counselors to deliver high\-quality programs while maintaining fiduciary responsibility and oversight.

  



This position will support


**Essential Functions:**


* **Program Quality:** Provides oversight and coordinates high quality youth development programs in afterschool and outdoor day camp setting for assigned location(s), in adherence to Y program model, curriculum and quality standards. Ensure camp grounds are safe, engaging. Maintains professional and technical knowledge by attending the local and national camp standards trainings, seminars, webinars, conferences etc.
* **Program Compliance:** Assures compliance with state and local regulations as they relate to program areas. Ensures that YMCA program standards are met and risk management policies are followed.
* **Program Planning \& Innovation:** Assist staff in planning, organizing, and implementing age\-appropriate/developmentally appropriate program activities for afterschool programs and outdoor day camp. Fosters innovation by recognizing and sharing new and relevant ideas. Demonstrates courageous and intelligent risk taking.  

Staff Development: Recruits, hires, and trains required program staff needed to meet DFPS minimum licensing standards and Y staffing ratios. Reviews and evaluates staff performance and develops strategies to motivate staff to achieve program goals.
* **Fiscal Management:** Adheres to responsible fiscal stewardship, including managing all accounts receivables and accounts payable related to site budgets. Manage payroll related transactions and monitors for accuracy and completion. Ensures program operates within budget and meets fiscal goals through forecasting and budget monitoring.
* **Marketing \& Promotion:** Implements marketing strategies to promote program growth and brand exposure to the targeted communities and families. Expands program within the community in accordance with strategic and operating plans. Assists in the marketing and distribution of program information.
* **Community Engagement:** Builds and maintains relationship with families through consistent communication including parent orientations, newsletters, parent conferences and parent engagement events. Actively engages in school district and/or center\-based community events to build deeper relationships and YMCA brand awareness.
* **Center Engagement:** Actively participates in YMCA center activities, meetings, and staff trainings. Intentionally collaborates with center program staff. Develop and maintain good working relationships with the directors, staff, parents and families by way of open communication, positive problem solving, flexibility, and teamwork.
* **Relationship Building:** Develops and maintains strong relationships with school administration, parent and community groups and other organizations and agencies related to assigned programs. Responds to all agency, parent and community inquiries and complaints in a timely manner.
* **Program Evaluation:** Collects necessary qualitative and quantitative data needed to monitor program evaluation and measure program effectiveness. Lead Youth Program Quality (YPQ) intervention process at assigned locations. Use continuous program Monitors and evaluates the effectiveness of and participation in program and make adjustments to program as needed.
* **Annual Campaign:** Participates in the annual fundraising campaign and committees as assigned at the center and meets staff campaign goals. Supports direct staff in their participation in the Better Us annual fundraising campaign.
* **Project Teams:** Participates in project teams related to advancing the strategic vision and goals of youth development department.
* **Child Abuse Prevention:** Reports suspicious and inappropriate behaviors and policy violations. Follows mandated abuse and incident reporting requirements. Ensures youth are properly signed in and signed out, ensures only authorized adults are allowed in the facility, etc. Adheres to policies related to boundaries with each child.

  

**Job Requirements:**


* 3\-5 years previous experience including 2 years supervisory experience in licensed childcare, preferred
* Bachelor’s degree in related field or equivalent (preferred)
* Must meet Director qualifications as stated in the Minimum Standards (\#744\.1015\) under TDPRS.
* At least 21 years of age.
* CPR, First Aid, AED certifications and Child Abuse prevention trainings.
* Previous experience with diverse populations.
* Strong communication skills (verbal and written).
* Current record of tuberculosis examination and/or current local health department regulations, examinations, and/or immunization as required by the local or State Department of Health.
* Available for trainings on occasional weekends and after hours

  

**SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES**



This position has supervisory responsibilities.

  


**EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE**



Bachelor’s degree and/or equivalent

jobs.description:
**Location:** Albany, NY **Category:** Professional **Job Type:** Full\-time **Posted On:** Wed Jul 22 2026 **Job Description:**  

  

Since its founding in 1959, the Massry School of Business has been committed to providing high\-quality, high\-value business education to a diverse student population. As one of seven schools within the University at Albany \- one of four university centers in the State University of New York (SUNY) system \- the School serves approximately 2,284 undergraduate and 398 graduate students. Located in New York's Capital Region, the School is a recognized leader in business education and plays a vital role in advancing professional and management education regionally and globally.


The School's mission is grounded in expanding opportunity and advancing social mobility, preparing students from diverse backgrounds for meaningful careers and lifelong success. This commitment resonates deeply with alumni, corporate partners, and philanthropic supporters who are invested in shaping the next generation of business leaders.


The Massry School is experiencing significant philanthropic momentum. The current Inspire the Next campaign has surpassed its $30 million goal ahead of schedule, strengthening support for scholarships, faculty excellence, student success initiatives, and strategic priorities. Building on this success, the School is well positioned for continued growth in philanthropy and engagement.


The University at Albany seeks an accomplished and enterprising fundraising professional to serve as Senior Director of Development for the Massry School of Business. The senior director will be responsible for engaging high net\-worth alumni and friends and designing and executing solicitation strategies to secure gifts of $25,000\-$10,000,000\+.


The senior director will help provide the vision and leadership for fundraising efforts for the school's highest strategic priorities. This individual will collaborate with campus partners, in particular the dean, and develop coordinated strategies aimed at maximizing the lifetime engagement of a portfolio that includes many of UAlbany's most affluent and important prospects.


**Primary Responsibilities:**


* Manage a portfolio of high net\-worth individuals through the identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship cycles; forge relationships with a diverse population of prospects that will realize six\-, seven\-, and eight\-figure gifts and advance the Massry School to new levels of distinction.
* Meet with prospects, often in tandem with the president, dean, and faculty members; work proactively to deepen prospects' engagement through substantive campus visits and other strategies.
* Lead and execute advancement strategies for the Massry School, supporting the final phase of the School's $30 million *Inspire the Next* campaign.
* Collaborate closely with the dean to identify and articulate her/his fundraising priorities.
* Oversee the preparation of proposals, talking points, briefings for president, dean, and faculty members; ensure that all stakeholders are involved in key efforts focused on specific prospects.
* Supervise and manage one direct report, the associate director of development, providing guidance, support and performance feedback to assure goals, programs, activities and personnel practices are consistent with and contribute to the University's mission. Lead employee for maximum performance and dedication, fostering a positive and productive work environment. Complete performance management feedback and goals timely and per requirements, offering constructive feedback and developing improvement plans.
* Collaborate with the associate vice president for planned giving to design prospect strategies for complex solicitations and identify appropriate giving vehicles.
* Work with donor relations colleagues to ensure that donors are properly stewarded.
* Collaborate with the dean to manage the dean's advisory board, identifying and recruiting new members, and creating strategies and initiatives to deepen the board's engagement.
* Support donor and alumni engagement events through planning, outreach, and execution.
* Maintain complete and timely records of contacts with prospects.
* Other reasonable duties as assigned.


**Functional and Supervisory Relationships:**


* Reports to: Associate Vice President for Advancement
* Supervises the following positions: Associate Director of Development


**Job Requirements:**


* Strong communication skills: oral, written, and listening.
* Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work across a complex institution.
* The ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
* Ability to connect and partner with a range of people, including senior staff, donors, and volunteers.
* Ability to work with diverse constituencies and foster inclusion.
* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail are essential.
* Willingness and ability to travel to meet with donors.
* Applicants must demonstrate an ability to develop inclusive and equitable relationships within our diverse campus community.
* Applicants must demonstrate an ability to support diversity, equity, access, inclusion, and belonging relative to their role.

This historic, Ivy League school consistently ranks among the top 10 universities in the annual U.S. News \& World Report survey. Penn has 12 highly\-regarded schools that provide opportunities for undergraduate, graduate and continuing education, all influenced by Penn’s distinctive interdisciplinary approach to scholarship and learning. jobs.description:
**MISISON STATEMENT**


The Fiesta® San Antonio Commission Inc. is a nonprofit, volunteer, self\-supporting organization that plans, promotes, coordinates, and preserves Fiesta® San Antonio through support of its Participating Member Organizations in presenting a safe, educational, annual celebration of the diverse heritage, culture and spirit of San Antonio for its residents and visitors.  



**WHAT IS FIESTA® SAN ANTONIO**


Fiesta® San Antonio started in 1891 as a one\-parade event to honor the memory of the heroes of the Alamo and the Battle of San Jacinto. That historic commemoration still takes place, but for more than a century, Fiesta® has grown into a celebration of San Antonio’s rich and diverse cultures and today is one of this nation’s premier festivals with an economic impact of more than $340 million.  



Each year, more than one hundred San Antonio Area Non\-Profit, Fiesta Member Organizations coordinate more than one hundred Official Fiesta® Events to raise funds to provide services to San Antonio citizens throughout the year.



The Fiesta San Antonio Commission’s purpose is to help each of its Member Organizations be as successful as they can be.



The Fiesta San Antonio Commission proudly supports Fiesta Member Organizations that support:


* Higher Education Scholarships and Programs
* Diverse Cultures
* Historical Significance / The Alamo
* Arts / Nature
* Military Programs
* Children’s Events / Programs
* Pets / Animal Welfare
* Inclusive Events / LGBTQ
* Sports / Health and Wellness
* Special Needs Community
* Older Adult Community
* Local Community Building


**POSITION SUMMARY**


The Director of Development leads a comprehensive fundraising strategy in support of Fiesta San Antonio, one of the nation’s premier cultural celebrations. This role is responsible for generating revenue through emerging philanthropic initiatives, major gifts, grants, and corporate sponsorships, while building long\-term sustainability through planned giving and capital campaign development.  



Working closely with the Executive Director, Executive Committee, Board of Directors and the Development Committee, the Director of Development will drive strategies that strengthen revenue, deepen partnerships, and position the Commission for future growth.  



**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**


* Fundraising \& Sponsorship Strategy


* Develop and execute strategies to create endowments and grow major gifts, grants, annual giving, corporate sponsors and event revenue
* Identify new partnership opportunities and secure multi\-year commitments
* Identify and secure transformational and long\-term funding opportunities
* Meet or exceed annual fundraising goals


* Grants \& Institutional Funding


* Identify, pursue, and secure foundation and grant funding aligned with Fiesta’s mission and programs
* Build and manage a grants pipeline, including proposal development, submission, and reporting
* Establish relationships with institutional funders to support both programmatic and strategic initiatives


* Planned Giving \& Long\-Term Philanthropy


* Develop and launch a planned giving program to support long\-term sustainability
* Create opportunities for legacy and estate giving, including donor education and recognition
* Integrate planned giving into overall donor engagement and stewardship strategies


* Capital Campaign Development


* Support the planning and early development of a capital campaign or major fundraising initiative
* Build a pipeline of leadership\-level donors and prospects
* Partner with leadership and board members to advance campaign strategy and readiness


* Relationship Management


* Manage a portfolio of donors, prospects and sponsors
* Cultivate, solicit, and steward high\-level relationships
* Create customized engagement opportunities aligned with donor and sponsor interests


* Membership Integration \& Growth


* Evaluate how fundraising efforts can support and enhance Membership growth and engagement
* Develop strategies to better integrate fundraising and membership pathways
* Identify opportunities to convert members into donors and sponsors into long\-term partners


* Event \& Community Engagement


* Lead fundraising efforts tied to Fiesta events
* Represent the Commission at events, sponsor activities, and community functions
* Partner with member organizations to align fundraising and engagement opportunities


* Board \& Leadership Collaboration


* Work with the Development Committee Volunteers to create fundraising goals and strategies
* Engage Board members in fundraising strategy, donor introductions, and campaign development
* Provide regular updates on pipeline, revenue, grants activity, and strategic initiatives


* Stewardship \& Reporting


* Ensure donors and sponsors are recognized and all benefits are fulfilled
* Track activity, pipeline, and revenue in CRM systems
* Maintain accurate reporting across philanthropic giving, grants, and sponsorships


* Success Measures


* Growth in total fundraising revenue, including grants and major gifts
* Increase in multi\-year and larger sponsorship commitments
* Establishment of a planned giving program and pipeline
* Progress toward capital campaign readiness and leadership gifts
* Strong donor, sponsor, and member retention and engagement
* Increased alignment between membership and fundraising strategies

  


**REQUIREMENTS**


* Bachelor’s degree required
* 7\+ years of experience in fundraising, sponsorships, or business development
* Proven success securing major gifts
* Proven success securing corporate sponsorships
* Strong relationship\-building, communication, and presentation skills

* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Exceptional organization skills
* Ability to work with people from diverse backgrounds
* Enthusiastic and positive attitude toward helping others

**About Mesa**

Founded in 2020, Mesa is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preventing homelessness among young adults age 18\-24 in Ventura County. We provide transitional housing and trauma\-informed supportive services to help participants stabilize their lives, gain independence, and develop the skills necessary for long\-term success. Our holistic services help them to reach their goals in health, academic achievement, vocational skills, relationship building, community engagement \- and to secure permanent housing. Our mission is to cultivate possibility for young adults facing homelessness. Mesa began serving participants on August 1st, 2024, and is currently home to 13 young adults.

**Position Summary**

The Development Director provides strategic leadership and hands\-on execution of all fundraising efforts to sustain and grow Mesa’s mission and to achieve the annual fundraising revenue goals. This person is responsible for designing and implementing a comprehensive development strategy that includes individual giving, major gifts, government, corporate and foundation grants, sponsorships, events, planned giving, endowment, and donor stewardship. They work closely with the Executive Director, Board, staff, and volunteers to achieve long\-term revenue growth and advance Mesa’s sustainability.

**Primary Responsibilities**

**Fundraising Strategy \& Leadership**

* Develop, execute, and evaluate a comprehensive fundraising plan to meet fundraising revenue goals.
* Establish multi\-year fundraising strategies with associated timelines.
* Collaborate with leadership on financial planning, forecasting, revenue diversification, and organizational priorities

**Donor Cultivation, Stewardship, \& Major Gifts**

* Identify, cultivate, and solicit major gifts.
* Implement stewardship strategies to increase retention and lifetime giving.
* Partner with the Executive Director and Board to engage donors effectively.

**Grants \& Institutional Giving**

* Develop a grant strategy, pipeline, and calendar to secure grants from foundations, corporations, and government agencies.
* Lead grant writing, proposal/LOI submission, and reporting with some grant writer support; partner with Finance Director on reporting.
* Build and steward relationships with program officers, fund representatives, and institutional partners.
* Track requirements, deliverables, and compliance across all awarded funds, coordinating with finance and programs.

**Development Operations \& Supervision**

* Supervise the Development Manager in support of achieving the annual fundraising plan.
* Ensure accurate CRM use, forecasting, reporting, performance metrics, and donor data integrity.
* Provide reports and presentations to the Executive Director, Board, Board Committees, and others, as required.

**Communications \& Marketing Alignment**

* Manage messaging and development of donor\-facing materials, including appeals, event collaterals, digital campaigns, print and digital collaterals, and website content.
* Partner with marketing/communications staff to strengthen brand awareness and storytelling aligned with Mesa’s mission.

**Events**

* Lead all aspects of fundraising and cultivation events, including concept development, sponsorship strategy, and donor experience.
* Create strategy for events to ensure they advance donor pipeline priorities and contribute meaningfully to revenue and relationship goals.
* Recruit and lead events committees.

**Board \& Executive Engagement**

* Provide development updates at Board meetings.
* Support Board member fundraising engagement and training.

**Organizational Collaboration \& Representation**

* Partner across departments to align fundraising with programs, operations, and finance.
* Inspire a shared belief in philanthropy, equipping staff and Board to participate in fundraising.
* Represent Mesa at external events, speaking engagements, and networking opportunities.

**Other**

* Uphold Mesa’s values: integrity, excellence, equity, respect, kindness, collaboration.
* Perform other duties as assigned.

**Qualifications**

**Required**

* 10\+ years nonprofit fundraising experience, including major gifts and grant writing (both foundation and government grants).
* Strategic thinking: Ability to develop and execute long\-term fundraising plans and understand donor motivations.
* Leadership: Experience managing teams, leading fundraising innovation, and building a strong organizational vision. Ability to inspire staff and volunteers.
* Data analysis: Proficiency in analyzing data to track trends, measure success, and inform strategy.
* Excel Proficiency: ability to create, manage, track spreadsheets and complex data.
* CRM experience (DonorPerfect, or similar).
* Financial acumen: Understanding financial principles and stewardship to manage the organization's resources and development budget effectively.
* Exceptional communication: Ability to articulate a mission compellingly through written and verbal channels, including grant writing and public speaking.
* Networking, stewardship and donor relations: Strong ability to build and maintain relationships with a wide range of stakeholders, including major donors, board members, and foundations.
* Demonstrated track record of fundraising success, including securing major gifts, planned giving, and grants (both government and foundation).
* Minimum of Bachelor's degree.
* High ethical standards.

**Preferred**

* Experience with youth services, housing, or social impact programs.
* Familiarity with Ventura County philanthropic landscape.
* Experience working with Marketing teams or leading Marketing efforts

**Physical Demands**:

* Ability to lift up to 30 pounds for event setup and materials handling.
* Ability to sit for one hour, minimum.
* Visual and audio acuity.
* Occasional evening and

jobs.title: Associate Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
### **We’re Looking for You:**

Have you ever asked yourself, “How can I make a difference?”, but you don’t know where to go or have the time to do it? Welcome to The Nature Conservancy.


You have found the solution and now you can pointedly make a difference every day! By joining our Kentucky team, you will become part of a group that is a force for nature and people. Our team consists of a dedicated group who are leading the conservation effort on multiple fronts and who expertly bring strategy and action together. We’re looking for an individual with fundraising or business development experience who wants to make a difference. The Conservancy is a global conservation leader and one of the top fundraising charities in the United States.


### **What We Can Achieve Together:**

The Associate Director of Philanthropy (ADOP) works closely with the philanthropy team to implement and coordinate effective multi\-year strategies and plans for gift prospects, including corporate, foundation, and individual donors. TNC is embarking on its largest fundraising campaign to date in support of our 2030 goals. From reforestation, land protection, and fire management in eastern Kentucky to regenerative agriculture and wetland restoration in western Kentucky, The Nature Conservancy is building a brighter future and boosting resiliency for nature and people in the Commonwealth.


The ADOP is responsible for building a portfolio of qualified donors and developing relationships. Relationship\-based fundraising is our approach, and they will positively connect and engage with their teammates, prospects, donors, and stakeholders. They understand and apply the principles of developing donor strategies. They will focus on providing the best\-in\-class donor stewardship and thrive on connecting and qualifying discovery prospects. They will be responsible for direct asks in support of global, regional, and local priorities to meet funding needs. They listen for opportunities for gifts of assets or other non\-cash gifts. They are energetic and focus on the daily work habits that are the foundations for success. They involve their Kentucky colleagues, Development Committee, Board of Trustees, and other natural partners, keeping them apprised of interactions, issues or concerns, and direct questions as appropriate. They can communicate the TNC programs in broad and compelling terms. The ADOP will be required to use TNC's Blackbaud donor database and participate in developing proposals. This position would require regular travel in Kentucky, focusing on particular geographies for donor meetings.


### **Responsibilities and Scope:**

* Manage a portfolio of 75\-100 donors, including donors who can give over $100,000 once the portfolio is developed. Raise an annual fundraising goal of $500,000 or more, in addition to a goal for bequest notifications and legacy gifts.
* Conduct 400 moves and interactions during a Fiscal Year (based on the assumption of a portfolio of 100 donors), with special attention to cultivating relationships with new prospects to the organization.
* Manage and implement strategic and highly curated events and/or donor engagement opportunities.
* Develop personalized written proposals and submit grant proposals, as needed.
* Act independently and in the supervisor’s stead as needed, and exercise independent judgment to identify and solve problems.
* Decisions may have a program\-wide impact and may bind the organization financially or legally.
* Demonstrate sensitivity in handling confidential information.
* Ensure compliance with TNC policies and procedures and external (donor/legal/IRS) requirements.
* Financial responsibility includes working within a budget and meeting fundraising objectives.
* Manage assigned projects, coordinating the work of peers and setting deadlines.
* May manage or participate in complex or sensitive negotiations.
* Convey the mission of TNC to diverse groups essential to the organization's overall prosperity.
* Travel (mostly within Kentucky) and work flexible hours as needed.
* Work environment involves only infrequent exposure to disagreeable elements and minor physical exertion and/or strain.
### **What You’ll Bring:**

* Bachelor's degree and 5 years of related experience or an equivalent combination.
* Experience building and maintaining long\-term relationships with fundraising constituents such as major donors, foundations, and corporations.
* Experience in asking for and closing gifts of $25,000 or more.
* Experience in managing and tracking multiple prospects and donors.
* Experience working with cross\-functional teams.
* Experience, coursework, or other training in fundraising principles and practices.

**Desired Qualifications:**

* Ability to determine an individual's interests, capacity, and potential for helping TNC meet its goals and act appropriately to tie those interests with our work.
* Ability to educate and inform prospective and existing donors about appropriate giving vehicles.
* Ability to implement and manage fundraising plans, including individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship plans.
* Proven track record and experience with securing annual gifts, corporate philanthropy, campaigns, major gifts, and planned gifts.
* Experience in event planning and curated donor stewardship events.
* Demonstrated experience using listening, diplomacy, and tact to build strong relationships and motivate donors.
* Strong writing skills for grants and personalized proposal development
* Proven ability to negotiate sensitive philanthropic agreements.

This position requires a valid driver's license and compliance with **TNC’s Auto Safety Program** . Employees may not drive Conservancy\-owned/leased vehicles, rental cars, or personal vehicles on behalf of the Conservancy if considered "high risk drivers." To learn more about the Auto Safety Program, visit the Auto Safety information page . Employment in this position will be contingent upon com

### **Description**


The **Vice President for Advancement \& Executive Director of the Hill\-Murray Foundation** serves as the school's chief advancement officer and a member of the President's Leadership Team. This executive provides visionary leadership for all advancement and Foundation activities, including fundraising, alumni and constituent engagement, advancement operations, communications, donor stewardship, and Foundation governance. Working closely with the President, the Vice President develops and executes a comprehensive advancement strategy that inspires philanthropy, strengthens lifelong relationships with alumni and friends, and secures the financial resources necessary to advance Hill\-Murray's long\-term mission and strategic priorities.


Serving concurrently as Executive Director of the Hill\-Murray Foundation, the Vice President partners closely with the Foundation Board of Governors to provide strategic advancement leadership, governance support, organizational management, and fundraising counsel. This individual ensures that the Foundation effectively advances the school's philanthropic priorities while fostering strong collaboration among the Foundation Board, Board of Trustees, President, and school leadership.


The Vice President leads and develops a high\-performing advancement team responsible for leadership giving, annual giving, advancement operations, donor stewardship, communications, alumni engagement, and fundraising events. Through collaborative leadership, strategic planning, and clear accountability, this individual builds a culture of philanthropy, innovation, service, and operational excellence while ensuring every donor and volunteer experiences Hill\-Murray with professionalism, gratitude, and care.


The successful candidate is a visionary leader, accomplished fundraiser, exceptional relationship builder, and trusted advisor with a demonstrated ability to cultivate transformational philanthropic partnerships, inspire generosity, develop high\-performing teams, and build an advancement program comparable to those of the nation's leading independent schools and small colleges. The candidate will be able to develop and execute a multi\-year advancement plan aligned with Hill\-Murray's Strategic Plan. Establish annual fundraising goals, measurable performance indicators, and accountability systems while establishing clear goals, performance expectations, and fostering a collaborative, mission\-driven culture.


All Hill\-Murray employees are expected to support, model, and champion HM’s mission statement: *Hill\-Murray empowers students to reach their fullest potential by inspiring them to utilize their God\-given strengths to become leaders who will make a positive difference in the world.*


**Primary Responsibilities:**


***Strategic Leadership for All Advancement and Foundation activities, including:***


* Comprehensive fundraising strategy
* Leadership, major, principal, and planned giving
* Annual Fund
* Capital and endowment campaigns
* Alumni and constituent engagement
* Donor stewardship

***Executive Director, Hill\-Murray Foundation***


Serve as Executive Director of the Hill\-Murray Foundation by providing strategic leadership and operational support to the Foundation Board, including:


* Partnering with the Foundation Board Chair to establish strategic priorities and annual goals.
* Supporting board recruitment, orientation, education, and committee effectiveness.
* Preparing board agendas, reports, and fundraising updates.
* Managing Foundation operations, budgets, policies, and governance.
* Ensuring effective communication between the Foundation Board, President, and Board of Trustees.

***Philanthropic Leadership***


Lead Hill\-Murray's major, principal, capital, endowment, corporate, and planned giving efforts and personally manage the school’s highest\-capacity donors and benefactors by:


* Identifying, cultivating, soliciting, and stewarding transformational gifts.
* Partnering with the President, Foundation Board members, and volunteer leaders on significant gift opportunities.
* Building relationships with alumni, parents, grandparents, foundations, corporations, and community leaders while creating meaningful opportunities for donors to invest in HM.

***Advancement Team Leadership***


Lead, mentor, and develop a high\-performing Advancement Office committed to excellence, accountability, collaboration, and innovation, while directly supervising:


* Director of Leadership Giving
* Leadership Giving Officer
* Director of Alumni
* Annual Giving \& Alumni Manager
* Database \& Donor Relations Manager
* Communications \& Marketing Manager
* Volunteers and Advancement Support Staff

* Ensure every donor experiences exceptional stewardship through meaningful recognition, personalized communication, and opportunities to witness the impact of their generosity.

* Oversee advancement operations, including Salesforce, prospect research, gift administration, reporting, fundraising analytics, and data gov

jobs.title: Executive Director of Development, School of Education
jobs.description:
**Company Description**  

The Indiana University Foundation (IUF) advances the mission of Indiana University by inspiring philanthropy that fuels student success, pioneering research, and transformational impact across Indiana and beyond.


At IUF, we connect donors to the causes they care about most—turning passion into purpose and generosity into lasting impact. Our team values collaboration, innovation, data informed strategy, and meaningful relationships, all within a culture that supports professional growth and mission‑driven work.

 **Job Description**  

The Indiana University School of Education is at the exciting inflection point of reimagining how education transforms lives while launching bold, interdisciplinary initiatives that address some of society's most pressing challenges. As Executive Director of Development, you will partner directly with the Dean to shape the future of one of the nation's leading schools of education, building philanthropic support for groundbreaking priorities including a new Center for Education Innovation and Entrepreneurship, emerging research and outreach opportunities in the autism and neurodiversity space, innovative health education partnerships, collaborative initiatives with schools and units across Indiana University—including IU Innovates—to accelerate innovation and expand impact, and enhanced pathways for aspiring educators.


This is a rare opportunity to help define what's next while contributing to Indiana University's historic Never Daunted: Indiana University’s Campaign for the Future, one of the largest and most ambitious fundraising campaigns in higher education.


We're looking for a strategic and entrepreneurial advancement leader who sees possibility where others see roadblocks. The successful candidate will be energized by building partnerships across schools, campuses, and disciplines, uncovering new philanthropic opportunities, and helping donors think bigger about the impact they can make. You'll inspire a high\-performing team, cultivate transformational relationships with alumni, volunteers, faculty, and campus leaders, and create a culture where innovation, collaboration, and bold ideas flourish. If you're a visionary relationship builder who isn't afraid to challenge convention, explore new fundraising models, and champion initiatives that improve education for generations to come, we invite you to join us.


Knowledge \& Skills:

* Demonstrated success securing major and principal gifts and leading comprehensive fundraising strategies.
* Proven ability to think strategically and creatively, identifying innovative philanthropic opportunities and building support for transformational initiatives.
* Experience fostering collaboration across campuses, schools, academic units, and advancement disciplines to achieve shared goals.
* Exceptional relationship\-building skills with donors, volunteers, faculty, senior administrators, and community partners.
* A visionary leadership style with the ability to build culture, develop talent, and inspire high\-performing teams.
* Experience leading fundraising efforts within a large, complex, matrixed organization.
* Strong strategic planning, analytical, and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and drive measurable results.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills with the ability to engage diverse audiences.
* Demonstrated commitment to inclusive leadership and building authentic partnerships.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.
* Eight or more years of progressively responsible fundraising experience, including success securing major and principal gifts.
* Four or more years of progressive leadership and people management experience.

  

**Additional Information**  

Great fundraising starts with great stories, and yours is no exception. Along with your resume, we strongly encourage you to submit a cover letter that tells us about the vision you would bring to the IU School of Education, a challenge you've transformed into opportunity, and why you're **Never Daunted** when it comes to creating lasting impact. After all, the next transformational gift often begins with someone willing to imagine what others haven't. This position will be posted through Wednesday, August 12th. The compensation range for this position is $120,000\- $125,000\.

**The IUF is committed to providing a safe, respectful, and professional work environment that is free of Discrimination and Harassment. The IUF will not tolerate any form of Discrimination or Harassment based on the Individual’s race, ethnicity, religion, color, sex, age, national origin, genetic information, sexual orientation, disability, gender identity or expression, ancestry, marital status, protected veteran status, pregnancy, or any other basis prohibited by law.**

Oversee the clinical, financial, and administrative operations of specified programs. Ensure proper standards and regulations are adhered to as set forth by the funding sources. Ensure programs meet the quality standards and provide value to the public.

* Direct and manage the activities of program staff. Hire staff appropriate to the position. Utilize staff appropriately with the goal of optimizing effort and achieving operational and financial goals.
* Manage staff performance. Provide direction, training, and coaching; implement recognition and rewards programs; conduct performance planning and review sessions; provide guidance on career development. Responsible for counseling and corrective action programs, as necessary.

Assist Managers in completing comprehensive assessment for persons served. Participate in various internal and external meetings regarding treatment/support planning.

* Foster teamwork and leadership in work groups.

 **Preferred /Required Education:**  

A high school diploma or equivalent is required; Master’s degree in Human Services, Psychology, or related. In some cases, experience may be substituted for academic training.

WHO WE ARE \& WHAT WE DO:
**Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare (TMH)** is a private, nonprofit community\-based healthcare system that provides advanced care to a 21\-county region in North Florida and South Georgia. We are a career destination with nearly 6,000 colleagues who reflect the diversity of our community.  

TMH is the region’s healthcare leader and top provider of advanced care with a 772\-bed acute care hospital and the region’s only:* Level II Trauma Center
* Joint Commission Comprehensive Stroke Center
* Level III Neonatal Intensive Care
* Pediatric Intensive Care
* The most advanced cancer, heart and vascular, orthopedic \& surgery programs in the Panhandle


Our system also includes a psychiatric hospital, multiple specialty care centers, four residency programs and nearly 40 affiliated physician practices.
HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT:
**Location:** Tallahassee, FL  

As the **Executive Director of IS Operations and Technology**, you will play a pivotal role in the operational integrity, reliability, and continuous improvement of the health system's technology infrastructure and IS services. Serves as a key partner to clinical and business leaders; balances day\-to\-day operational excellence with strategic infrastructure planning to support a safe, efficient, and patient\-centered care environment.  

If you are a seasoned IT leader with a passion for technology innovation and a deep understanding of healthcare IT, we invite you to apply for this exciting opportunity! Join our dedicated team in using technology to revolutionize healthcare and positively impact patient outcomes.  

 The **Executive Director of IS Operations and Technology** is responsible for:* Demonstrating integrity, compassion, accountability, respect, and excellence (I CARE values).
* Communicates information timely via appropriate channels.
* Displays an understanding of diversity.
* Manages departmental budget.
* Meet goals/expectations established over the previous year.
* Manages human resources.
* Meets or exceeds all The Joint Commission (TJC) requirements.
* Solves problems; recommends sound solutions.
* Enhances and contributes to a safe environment for all colleagues, visitors, and patients.
* Oversees all “Keep the Lights On” (KTLO)/“Run the Business” (RTB) activities; ensures clinical, administrative, and operational information systems remain available, secure, and performant 24 hours/day, 7days/week (“24/7”).
* Collaborates with the security team to mitigate cybersecurity threats.
* Leads and develops a team of IS managers, engineers, and support staff members.
* Performs miscellaneous duties.

**Reports to:*** Chief Information Officer/VP

**Supervises:*** Director/IT Infrastructure Services
* Director/IT Planning and Quality


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO APPLY:
**Education:*** Required: Bachelor’s degree in information technology, computer science, or a related healthcare field;
* Preffered: Master's degree.

**Experience:*** Minimum of ten (10\) years of healthcare IT and operations experience (including five (5\) years of supervisory or managerial experience).

**Certification/License/Registry:**

* Required: None.

**In****centives:**


We’re thankful for what you do for us. *Here’s what we can do for you:*

* This position is performance based annual bonus eligible
* Sign\-on bonus and transition allowance eligible\*\*\*
* Health Insurance (Medical, Dental \& Vision)
* Retirement Contributions Matching Up to 6% \[401(a)/403(b)]
* Annual Raise\*\*\*
* Tuition Reimbursement\*\*\*
* Scholarship Opportunities\*\*\*
* Paid Time Off
* Childcare (TMH owned onsite facility)
* Premier Health and Fitness Club (TMH owned offsite facility)
* Wellness Programs and Colleague Discounts


\*\*\*Restrictions may apply  

**Additional Information:*** Only candidates who meet the requirements will be contacted for further consideration.
* This vacancy is not eligible for sponsorship. TMH will not sponsor or transfer visas for this position.
* :

*Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare is an equal opportunity employer and drug\-free workplace. All employment is contingent upon successful completion of a drug screen, background check, reference verification, health assessment, and credential/license verification.*

new_jobs.title: Deputy Director
new_jobs.description:
**Overview**

For 50 years, Commonweal has worked at the intersection of health, healing, education, environmental stewardship, and social change. Through a diverse portfolio of programs, we bring together researchers, practitioners, artists, educators, healthcare professionals, environmental leaders, and community partners to address some of today's most complex challenges.

Just as important as what we do is how we work.

At Commonweal, we value curiosity, thoughtful leadership, and genuine collaboration. We believe the strongest organizations are built on trust, respect, open communication, and a willingness to learn from one another. Our staff work across disciplines and programs, supporting each other's success while remaining deeply committed to our individual missions. We strive to create an environment where people are encouraged to contribute ideas, take initiative, build strong relationships, and do meaningful work alongside colleagues who care deeply about making a difference.

As we continue to grow and evolve, we're seeking an experienced **Deputy Director** to help lead Commonweal's next chapter.

Reporting to the Executive Director, the Deputy Director serves as Commonweal's senior internal leader and strategic partner, providing leadership across finance, human resources, operations, technology, administration, organizational development, and program support. This role helps strengthen organizational systems, develop leaders, improve operational effectiveness, and ensure that Commonweal's infrastructure supports the important work happening across our programs.

We're looking for someone who:

* Has significant senior leadership experience managing complex organizations or departments.
* Brings strong operational, financial, and organizational management skills.
* Is an experienced people leader who enjoys coaching others and building high\-performing teams.
* Thinks strategically while remaining practical and solution\-oriented.
* Is comfortable leading organizational change while preserving a collaborative and respectful culture.
* Values relationships, clear communication, and thoughtful decision\-making.
* Is excited by the opportunity to help a mission\-driven nonprofit strengthen its organizational capacity for the future.

Why Commonweal?

This is an opportunity to help lead an organization with an extraordinary history and an ambitious future. You'll partner closely with the Executive Director and work alongside dedicated colleagues who are committed to improving health, protecting the environment, and advancing social change. You'll have the opportunity to shape systems, strengthen leadership, and help ensure that Commonweal continues to thrive for decades to come.

Position Details

* **Location:** Hybrid. Regular on\-site work at Commonweal's headquarters in Bolinas, California, with flexibility for remote work when appropriate.
* **Salary:** $175,000–$205,000 annually, depending on experience.
* **Status:** Full\-time, Regular, Exempt.
* **Benefits:** Comprehensive benefits package including employer\-sponsored health, dental, vision, life and long\-term disability insurance; generous paid time off; retirement benefits; and additional benefits based on eligibility.

**If you're inspired by Commonweal's mission and excited to help strengthen an organization where thoughtful leadership, collaboration, and service are central to our culture, we'd love to hear from you.** 

View the complete Job Description: Deputy Director, Commonweal

https://drive.google.com/file/d/15f9BQGvo0kLteTPxQTyqoVS7IVS5kvE\_/view

To apply, please send your resume and a cover letter to jobs@commonweal.org.

Pay: $175,000\.00 \- $205,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 403(b)
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible schedule
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Bolinas, CA 94924

new_jobs.title: Executive Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
**About Destination Tomorrow** 

Destination Tomorrow (DT) is a national grassroots LGBTQ\+ organization founded in the Bronx, built on the principle that no individual should have to leave their neighborhood for necessary services. DT's mission is to move clients off the path of emergency care to one where they can grow, learn, and flourish by emphasizing economic, social, and mental empowerment through comprehensive educational, financial, and health programs, as well as housing solutions. As an expanding multi\-site organization, Destination Tomorrow proudly serves the community across The Bronx, New York City; Atlanta, GA; and Washington, D.C.

**Position Summary**

**The Executive Director of Operations (ED Ops)** is Destination Tomorrow's enterprise infrastructure leader, translating strategic vision into operational architecture—aligning the executive team and building the internal capacity, accountability systems, analytics capabilities, compliance and risk governance frameworks necessary to support enterprise\-wide excellence and sustainable expansion. As the agency grows, the ED Ops ensures that workforce systems and process discipline shift from ad hoc execution to structured, repeatable, and enforceable.

Building on the agency's existing design, under the direction of the CEO and CSO, the ED Ops develops a cross\-agency operational framework that replicates the DT Community Model across office locations and client sites. Specific requirements for housing plug into this framework. Day\-to\-day housing operations remain with Social Services leadership.

**Core Responsibilities**

**Executive Operating Model \& Meeting Cadence** 

* Establish and facilitate the enterprise executive operating rhythm, eliminating fragmentation across verticals and ensuring infrastructure planning aligns with strategic initiatives, revenue campaigns, and major organizational milestones.
* Introduce standardized governance, reporting, and accountability processes across operational and programmatic leadership.
* Ensure executive dashboards include performance metrics, risk summaries, workforce indicators, and operational health signals, and are integrated into QBRs, Board reporting cycles, and enterprise risk reviews.
* Partner with the CEO, CSO, and Executive Director of Social Services (EDSS) to translate 3\- and 5\-year strategic plans into operational roadmaps covering staffing, facilities, technology, systems, and sustained budgeting.
* Partner with the EDSS to ensure enterprise systems, reporting structures, workforce infrastructure, and operational governance effectively support frontline program delivery and client services.

**Enterprise Operating Framework \& Organizational Scaling**

* Lead the operational framework for replication of the DT Community Model across new offices and client sites, ensuring standardized processes, staffing plans, and governance structures.
* Own and maintain the standardized enterprise core of DT's client site staff operating manuals — security, clinical operations, onboarding, and site procedures — into which each site integrates its approved protocol variations.
* Design and standardize core operational playbooks for repeatable agency processes such as new program launches, location openings, procurement cycles, IT deployments, and cross\-agency initiatives.
* Partner with the EDSS to operationalize scalable service delivery models while preserving program quality, compliance, and client experience during expansion.
* Manage corporate administrative facilities, leases, and enterprise physical infrastructure. (Housing program sites remain under Social Services leadership, with close coordination on enterprise standards, compliance, and facilities support.)
* Manage NYC and ATL office managers to promote consistent, professional, efficient office operations and employee and visitor experience.

**Risk, Compliance \& Legal** 

* Serve as primary Enterprise Risk Manager, owning the Enterprise Risk Register by consolidating risk inputs across the agency and external stakeholders, and maintaining visibility across financial, operational, programmatic, compliance, and reputational risks.
* Define and enforce a standardized enterprise risk rating methodology (e.g., likelihood × impact), ensuring consistency in reporting across all verticals.
* Establish and facilitate quarterly Enterprise Risk Reviews with executive leadership to evaluate risk exposure, mitigation strategies, and mitigation planning.
* Escalate material risks to the CEO and Board as appropriate, ensuring timely transparency and decision\-making.
* Oversee Compliance, ensuring regulatory adherence across all jurisdictions, including IT security, data privacy, facility safety, contract governance, and enterprise\-wide legal coordination.
* Oversee organizational insurance coverage, vendor risk assessments, procurement standards, and contract policies to ensure risk mitigation and operational consistency.

**Technology, Data \& Analytics**

* Oversee the enterprise IT strategy and manage the IT Lead, ensuring systems scalability, cybersecurity posture, CRM optimization, and technology alignment with organizational growth.
* Ensure analytics infrastructure supports enterprise governance needs, as well as program outcome measurement frameworks as defined by the EDSS and financial forecasting requirements defined by the CFO.
* Ensure data integrity, system alignment, and cross\-department reporting consistency across all locations and service lines.
* Manage the Data \& Analytics Lead, ensuring development and maintenance of agency\-wide dashboards, KPI tracking systems, and integrated reporting frameworks that support executive governance, risk review, financial forecasting, and program performance measurement.

**Workforce Infrastructure \& Organizational Capacity**

* Integrate workforce planning into operational scaling efforts, including new site launches, service expansions, and leadership successi

new_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
new_jobs.description:
**Chief Operating Officer**

Commerce CPA, LLC · New York, NY · Full\-Time Leadership Role

Commerce CPA is a fast\-growing CPA firm serving individuals and businesses across tax, bookkeeping, audit, payroll, and advisory. We are profitable, referral\-driven, and expanding quickly. To support the next stage of growth, we are hiring our first Chief Operating Officer to take full ownership of firm operations so the founder can focus exclusively on client development and growth.

**Why this role exists**

Our founder currently splits his time between two very different jobs: winning new business and running the entire operation. That split is now the single biggest constraint on our growth. Today the founder is pulled into day\-to\-day delivery — chasing client responses, confirming return statuses, reviewing work before it goes out, resolving software and workflow issues, and keeping deadlines from slipping. This role exists to change that permanently.

We are looking for a seasoned operations leader who has already done this at a larger firm and can step in to own every operational function end to end. The founder’s job is to bring in and sign clients. Your job is everything after that — delivering the work accurately, on time, and to a standard that keeps every client satisfied, while building the systems and team that let the firm scale without the founder’s involvement in operations.

**The mandate**

* **Take operations off the founder’s plate entirely.** Once a client is signed and engaged, you own the relationship’s delivery, quality, and outcome. The founder should not be the escalation path, the reviewer, or the person chasing status.
* **Scale revenue from \~$1M to $3–$5M within the next year** without a drop in quality, a loss of clients, or a capacity crisis. Build the operational engine that makes aggressive growth safe.
* **Protect and grow the client base.** Zero clients lost to responsiveness, missed deadlines, or avoidable errors. Client satisfaction is your primary scorecard.
* **Institutionalize how the firm runs.** Replace founder\-dependent, ad\-hoc processes with documented systems, clear ownership, and reliable execution across every function.

**What you will own**

Full operational accountability across all service lines and support functions:

**Service delivery — all functions**

* Tax (individual, business, multi\-state, crypto, and specialty filings), bookkeeping (monthly close, bank/feed reconciliations, financial statements), audit and attest engagements, payroll, and advisory — delivered accurately and on schedule.
* End\-to\-end workflow: engagement kickoff, document collection, preparation, review, filing/e\-file, and delivery to the client.
* A real quality\-control layer so nothing reaches a client without proper review — no missed filings, no unreviewed work, no re\-files.

**Client experience**

* Own client communication standards and response\-time SLAs across the whole team. Clients should never wait on a reply, and the founder should never have to prompt staff to respond.
* Complete visibility into every engagement’s status at any moment — what’s in progress, what’s pending, and what’s at risk — so nothing falls through the cracks.
* Manage escalations and turn service problems into retained, satisfied clients.

**Systems, process \& technology**

* Build, document, and enforce SOPs for every recurring process — onboarding, e\-file, follow\-ups, invoicing, client meetings, and file management — and keep them current.
* Own the tech stack and make it work as one system: TaxDome, the tax software (Drake / CCH), QuickBooks (Online and Desktop, including migrations), scheduling, and communication tools.
* Design workflows that are efficient and repeatable, drawing on what has worked at larger, more mature firms.

**Team leadership \& capacity**

* Lead, manage, and hold accountable the full delivery team (onshore and offshore) — tax, bookkeeping, audit, and administrative staff.
* Own capacity and busy\-season planning: staffing, workload balancing, and hiring so the firm can absorb rapid client growth without burnout or bottlenecks.
* Set performance expectations, run the daily/weekly operating cadence, and develop the team.

**Financial operations**

* Own invoicing, billing, and collections so revenue is captured and cash flows predictably.
* Track operational and delivery metrics, and report the health of the operation to the founder.

**What success looks like**

* **The founder is fully out of operations** — spending his time on sales and growth, not delivery or status\-chasing.
* **Growth target hit:** the firm scales toward $3–$5M with delivery quality intact.
* **Client retention and satisfaction rise;** churn from service issues goes to zero.
* **Deadlines are met reliably** with no missed or late filings.
* **The firm runs on documented systems** and a self\-sufficient team — not on the founder’s memory or intervention.

**First 12 months**

* **First 90 days:** learn the book of business and team, map every current process, and stand up a single source of truth for engagement status and client communication.
* **By 6 months:** documented SOPs live across all functions, a working quality\-review process, response\-time SLAs enforced, and the founder removed from day\-to\-day delivery.
* **By 12 months:** a scalable operating model and staffing plan proven to support $3–$5M, with clean handoffs from sales to delivery and no capacity crises.

**Who you are**

* **Proven COO / Director of Operations experience at a larger accounting or professional\-services firm** — you have run operations at scale and know what “good” looks like.
* **You have built and scaled operations before,** taking a firm through rapid growth while improving — not sacrificing — quality and client satisfaction.
* **Deep understanding of public\-accounting delivery:** tax, bookkeeping, audit, and the compliance calendar and deadlines that drive the work.
* **A systems buil

Thank you for considering a career with the Research Foundation of The City University of New York (RFCUNY).


The team at RFCUNY is made up of dedicated, talented professionals committed to providing the services that allow CUNY researchers, faculty, and staff to focus on their intellectual curiosity and scientific discoveries.


We are pleased that you are interested in exploring opportunities to join RFCUNY.


Primary Location:


OFFICE OF SR. UNIV DEAN FOR ACADEMIC AFFAIRS
Bargaining Unit:


No


The Creative Arts Team (CAT) is an arts\-in\-education program at the City University of New York (CUNY), serving more than 9,000 people annually in New York City. CAT uses theatre strategies to engage, inspire, and educate in a variety of settings, including schools, after\-school programs, college campuses, community\-based organizations, and government facilities.


CAT’s Learning Through Drama (LTD) team uses theme\-based, interactive drama and skill–building strategies to address social issues and behaviors that create challenges in everyday lives. The LTD team delivers programs to grades 6\-12, college students, and parents and adults. SEED programs address college readiness and retention, social\-emotional learning, conflict resolution, and skills for success in school and life. SEED programs provide a safe environment for participants to examine choices, decisions, and consequences. This process provides an opportunity to discover underlying motives, raise awareness, and provide solutions while promoting problem\-solving, decision\-making, critical thinking, and self\-advocacy skills to build confidence in achieving desired outcomes.


The Program Director reports to the Executive Director and provides strategic leadership, management, and oversight for a portfolio of programs. The Program Director develops curriculum; designs and delivers professional development training for teaching artists; and supervises project teams that may include full\-time staff, part\-time teaching artists, interns, and specialized consultants as needed. The position is responsible for understanding and ensuring compliance with all grants, contract, and award requirements, and for creating systems that support high\-quality program implementation in middle schools, high schools, and community settings. The Program Director oversees all aspects of program planning, implementation, staffing, partnerships, budgeting, evaluation, and reporting. The portfolio may include DYCD Crisis Management System and City Council\-funded initiatives, education programs, social\-emotional learning (SEL) professional development, grant\-funded workshops, and other strategic initiatives that advance the mission of the Creative Arts Team.

· Develop annual program structure and oversee implementation of all projects


· Directly supervise 6\-12 Actor Teachers, Associate Program director/s, and Program Manager/s

· Hire program staff, including organizing and hosting auditions and interviews as necessary, as well as onboarding paperwork in collaboration with colleagues at K\-16

· Provide (or delegate to Associate Program Director/s) ongoing task supervision for MSW candidates throughout the year to ensure they are having a meaningful internship, providing valuable services to the school community, and have the support they need.


· Develop relationships with organizations that specialize in violence interruption to provide Safe Passages as required by DYCD.


· Provide (or delegate to Associate Program Director/s) ongoing supervision for Safe Passages providers throughout the year to ensure healthy relationships with school communities, effective programming, and added safety of students.


· Maintain and develop program vision, purpose, and direction, collaborating with program staff and Executive Director.


· Train program staff in administrative systems


· Structure the regular collaborative creation of drama\-education curriculum with Actor Teachers


* Work with Executive Director to create annual program budget at the start of the fiscal year and manage the budget throughout the year

· Work with Development (and external evaluators when applicable) to design and implement program evaluations, including but not limited to student reflection surveys, teacher reflection surveys, and CAT staff reflection.


· Collaborate with development and program staff to ensure grant compliance, overseeing systems for reporting, deadlines, and documentation.

· Report on program work, in writing, upon agreed timelines.

new_jobs.description:
The Program Director of the Elder Justice Program is responsible for the overall leadership, administrative oversight, and supervision of the Elder Justice Program. This role ensures the delivery of high\-quality support services to clients and families served, compliance with all program standards, and adherence to contractual and regulatory requirements. The Program Director works closely with staff, community partners, funders, and agency leadership to support caregivers and strengthen the program’s impact.

**Organizational Values:** The Community Agency for Senior Citizens (CASC) assists Staten Islanders age 60 and older to live as independently as possible in the community. We do this by working in together with older adults, respecting their right to make decisions for themselves, honoring their life experiences and heritage, and recognizing the importance of friends, relatives and community in creating supportive environments for people as they age.

**Position Qualifications:**

· Master’s Degree required, including one of the following or an equivalent master’s\- level field:

· Social Work (MSW, LMSW or LCSW)

· Human services

· Social Services
